title: Elon Musk's Grok AI rolls out new fact-check tool can verify posts in seconds, but can it be trusted?
paragraph: Grok has rolled out a new feature allowing users to fact-check posts on X instantly. While the tool promises to fight misinformation, the chatbot’s past errors and AI hallucinations raise questions about its reliability.

title: Elon Musk's Grok AI rolls out new fact-check tool can verify posts in seconds, but can it be trusted?
paragraph: This photograph shows a smartphone bearing the logo of Grok, a generative artificial intelligence chatbot developed by US artificial intelligence company xAI in front of the X (formerly Twitter) logo in Brussels. (Photo: AFP)

title: Elon Musk's Grok AI rolls out new fact-check tool can verify posts in seconds, but can it be trusted?
paragraph: Grok, the artificial intelligence assistant created by Elon Musk’s xAI, has introduced a new feature designed to help users quickly verify information circulating on X. The update allows users to fact-check posts within seconds by tapping the Grok icon attached to a post.

title: Elon Musk's Grok AI rolls out new fact-check tool can verify posts in seconds, but can it be trusted?
paragraph: The move is part of the platform’s broader attempt to position AI as a tool to combat misinformation on social media. However, Grok’s history of controversial responses and factual mistakes is prompting debate over whether an AI chatbot can truly be trusted to act as a digital fact-checker.

title: Elon Musk's Grok AI rolls out new fact-check tool can verify posts in seconds, but can it be trusted?
paragraph: The new feature works in a simple way. Users can click on the Grok logo placed next to a post on X, and the chatbot will analyse the content before offering a quick explanation of whether the claim appears accurate or misleading.

title: Elon Musk's Grok AI rolls out new fact-check tool can verify posts in seconds, but can it be trusted?
paragraph: Musk confirmed the rollout in a post on X, explaining that tapping the Grok icon on the “left” side of a post would trigger the verification feature. But the announcement itself turned into an awkward moment for the platform.

title: Elon Musk's Grok AI rolls out new fact-check tool can verify posts in seconds, but can it be trusted?
paragraph: In a happy accident, Grok reportedly corrected the post, pointing out that the icon actually appears on the right side of the interface.

title: Elon Musk's Grok AI rolls out new fact-check tool can verify posts in seconds, but can it be trusted?
paragraph: While the exchange appeared light-hearted, it also highlighted the central concern surrounding the new feature. If AI is being positioned as a fact-checker, its own accuracy becomes critically important.

title: Elon Musk's Grok AI rolls out new fact-check tool can verify posts in seconds, but can it be trusted?
paragraph: The update also arrives only weeks after Grok faced intense criticism for generating explicit AI images involving women and children, raising questions about the safeguards built into the system.

title: Elon Musk's Grok AI rolls out new fact-check tool can verify posts in seconds, but can it be trusted?
paragraph: In our tests for the fact-check feature, as soon as you click on the logo, it describes the post in three points: The content, the caption and the engagement.

title: Elon Musk's Grok AI rolls out new fact-check tool can verify posts in seconds, but can it be trusted?
paragraph: The scepticism surrounding Grok’s fact-checking abilities stems largely from a series of controversial responses the chatbot produced in the past.

title: Elon Musk's Grok AI rolls out new fact-check tool can verify posts in seconds, but can it be trusted?
paragraph: Even before this feature existed, users on X had begun asking Grok to verify the truthfulness of posts or claims. In some cases the chatbot responded correctly, but several incidents showed how easily the system could veer off course.

title: Elon Musk's Grok AI rolls out new fact-check tool can verify posts in seconds, but can it be trusted?
paragraph: One of the most widely discussed episodes occurred last year when Grok began inserting references to the alleged “white genocide” in South Africa into unrelated discussions. Users asking about topics ranging from sports to finance suddenly received lengthy explanations about the controversial claim.

title: Elon Musk's Grok AI rolls out new fact-check tool can verify posts in seconds, but can it be trusted?
paragraph: In one instance, a user asked whether details about a baseball pitcher’s salary were accurate. Instead of addressing the sports query, Grok delivered a response discussing debates around violence against white farmers in South Africa.

title: Elon Musk's Grok AI rolls out new fact-check tool can verify posts in seconds, but can it be trusted?
paragraph: The responses appeared during a period when public figures including Musk and Donald Trump had been raising concerns about the issue. However, experts and South African officials have repeatedly said there is no evidence supporting claims of a state-organised genocide targeting white farmers.

title: Elon Musk's Grok AI rolls out new fact-check tool can verify posts in seconds, but can it be trusted?
paragraph: After the backlash, xAI said the behaviour was caused by an “unauthorised modification” to Grok’s prompt instructions that forced the chatbot to generate a specific political narrative. The company later promised to publish Grok’s prompts on GitHub and introduce stricter review processes to prevent similar changes.

title: Elon Musk's Grok AI rolls out new fact-check tool can verify posts in seconds, but can it be trusted?
paragraph: Another controversy erupted when Grok posted antisemitic remarks during an online exchange involving a social media account with the surname Steinberg. At one point, when asked which historical figure could deal with anti-white hatred, the chatbot named Adolf Hitler as the most effective option.

title: Elon Musk's Grok AI rolls out new fact-check tool can verify posts in seconds, but can it be trusted?
paragraph: The statement drew widespread condemnation because Hitler orchestrated the The Holocaust, during which around six million Jewish people were killed.

title: Elon Musk's Grok AI rolls out new fact-check tool can verify posts in seconds, but can it be trusted?
paragraph: Grok later called the posts “an unacceptable error from an earlier model iteration” and said it condemned Nazism unequivocally. xAI also said it removed the posts and implemented safeguards to block similar content.

title: Elon Musk's Grok AI rolls out new fact-check tool can verify posts in seconds, but can it be trusted?
paragraph: In artificial intelligence research, hallucinations refer to situations where a system generates information that sounds convincing but is actually incorrect or fabricated.

title: Elon Musk's Grok AI rolls out new fact-check tool can verify posts in seconds, but can it be trusted?
paragraph: Large language models such as Grok analyse patterns in enormous datasets and predict which words are likely to come next in a sentence. They do not independently verify facts or understand truth in the same way humans do.

title: Elon Musk's Grok AI rolls out new fact-check tool can verify posts in seconds, but can it be trusted?
paragraph: Because of this, the models can sometimes produce confident but inaccurate statements, combine unrelated pieces of information or invent details that do not exist.

title: Elon Musk's Grok AI rolls out new fact-check tool can verify posts in seconds, but can it be trusted?
paragraph: Hallucinations have been documented across multiple AI systems, including tools such as Google’s Gemini and OpenAI’s ChatGPT. Even advanced models occasionally produce fabricated sources, incorrect statistics or misleading explanations.

title: Elon Musk's Grok AI rolls out new fact-check tool can verify posts in seconds, but can it be trusted?
paragraph: This limitation raises concerns about relying on AI to verify information on social media. If the system misinterprets a post or hallucinates details, it could present incorrect conclusions while appearing authoritative.

title: Elon Musk's Grok AI rolls out new fact-check tool can verify posts in seconds, but can it be trusted?
paragraph: For now, AI fact-checking tools may work best as a starting point rather than a final verdict. Users may still need to cross-check claims with credible sources instead of relying solely on a chatbot’s judgement.

title: Elon Musk's Grok AI rolls out new fact-check tool can verify posts in seconds, but can it be trusted?
paragraph: As Grok’s new feature rolls out more widely, its performance will likely determine whether AI fact-checking becomes a trusted digital watchdog or just another layer in the misinformation debate.

title: India’s new AI content rules: What social media platforms must do and what changes for users
paragraph: The government has tightened the rules on how AI-generated content can appear on social media, putting new legal obligations on platforms to identify, label and, in some cases, block such material.

title: India’s new AI content rules: What social media platforms must do and what changes for users
paragraph: The changes, notified by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) and effective from February 20, amend the Information Technology (Intermediary Guidelines and Digital Media Ethics Code) Amendment Rules, 2026 and bring “synthetically generated information” into the compliance net.

title: India’s new AI content rules: What social media platforms must do and what changes for users
paragraph: The rules target synthetically generated content, audio, video or images created or altered using AI in a way that looks real and can mislead people.

title: India’s new AI content rules: What social media platforms must do and what changes for users
paragraph: The government has clarified that basic edits such as cropping, colour correction, translation, formatting, subtitles or accessibility improvements are not deepfakes. The focus is on fake or deceptive content that can cause harm.

title: India’s new AI content rules: What social media platforms must do and what changes for users
paragraph: If a platform knowingly allows harmful AI content or fails to act, it can be treated as not following due diligence rules.

title: India’s new AI content rules: What social media platforms must do and what changes for users
paragraph: India is one of the world's largest social media markets and a testing ground for generative AI products. As AI tools become common, the government wants to stop misuse before it spreads widely on social media.

title: India’s new AI content rules: What social media platforms must do and what changes for users
paragraph: The amendments come amid a surge in deepfakes and AI-generated content flooding the internet, from cloned celebrity voices and fabricated political videos to non-consensual intimate imagery.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: At the India AI Impact Summit 2026, companies and public institutions unveiled domain-specific AI systems embedded into telecom networks, payment rails, classrooms, healthcare platforms, agricultural supply chains and defence operations. The emphasis shifted away from abstract model benchmarks toward deployment inside high-scale, high-trust environments.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: While policy speeches outlined India’s AI ambitions, the product announcements showed where integration is already taking place.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Here’s what our correspondents, Chaitanya and Prabhanu, observed at the summit expo, including the systems demonstrated on the ground and the products launched alongside formal announcements.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Sarvam AI was among the most product-active companies during the summit period, announcing new foundation models while also expanding its deployment ecosystem across speech, vision, agents and infrastructure.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Sarvam introduced two large language models, including a 105 billion parameter model trained and hosted on India-based data centre infrastructure. A model of this scale places it closer to frontier-sized systems rather than lightweight fine-tuned models.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: The company also unveiled Indus, a chat-based application powered by the 105B model. Access to Indus remains limited due to compute capacity constraints.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Sarvam showcased AI dubbing, speech recognition and text-to-speech systems designed for multilingual Indian language use cases. These tools enable translation and voice generation across languages and are intended for media localisation, transcription and voice-based enterprise or public-sector interfaces.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: The company demonstrated document parsing and optical character recognition (OCR) systems capable of extracting text, tables and structured data from Indian language documents. It also presented an AI agent layer that reviews extracted text and corrects errors, allowing documents to be processed more accurately.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Sarvam unveiled Kaze, AI-powered smartglasses designed to run conversational workflows in a wearable format. The device supports offline functionality, meaning it can operate without continuous internet connectivity.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Sarvam introduced enterprise conversational agent platforms that can connect to backend systems to retrieve information or execute tasks. It also presented multi-agent coordination tools designed to allow several AI agents to work together on structured workflows.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: In addition, the company demonstrated on-device AI systems capable of operating in secure or air-gapped environments, meaning systems isolated from the public internet for security reasons.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Separately, Sarvam announced partnerships with Odisha and Tamil Nadu to build AI-optimised data centre infrastructure, linking its model development efforts with planned domestic compute expansion.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: BharatGen, the government-backed AI initiative, introduced Param2, a multimodal foundation model designed as part of India’s public digital AI infrastructure.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Param2 supports 22 Indian languages and is built to process multiple types of input, including text and images. By positioning it as public infrastructure, the initiative signals an intent to make the model accessible for integration into government services, research institutions and public-sector applications rather than limiting it to proprietary commercial use.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: The launch reflects an effort to develop a shared, state-supported AI backbone alongside private-sector model development.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Tech Mahindra announced a Hindi-first foundational large language model of approximately 8 billion parameters, tailored primarily for education-focused use cases.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Unlike larger frontier-scale models, this system is positioned as a more targeted deployment model. It is designed to support classroom AI agents, conversational tutoring and structured local-language learning systems.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: The focus on Hindi and educational contexts suggests an attempt to build domain-specific AI tools rather than compete directly in general-purpose model development.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Gnani.ai, an Indian speech technology startup focused on conversational and voice AI solutions, showcased its multilingual voice AI system at the summit. The company’s technology combines speech recognition, synthesis and voice cloning to support a range of use cases across enterprise and public-sector applications.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: At the event, Gnani.ai demonstrated capabilities that include text-to-speech synthesis in multiple Indian languages and voice cloning from short audio samples. The system can generate natural-sounding speech and replicate a speaker’s voice with minimal data.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Gnani.ai’s platform targets enterprise workflows such as customer support automation and language-inclusive voice services for government and public interfaces.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: In addition to product demonstrations, several companies announced institutional partnerships and long-term commitments at the India AI Impact Summit. These announcements focused on government collaboration, enterprise deployment and infrastructure expansion.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Google announced multiple partnerships involving Indian government and public-sector institutions, spanning research access, civil service training and climate policy.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Google DeepMind said it will collaborate with Indian government bodies and local institutions under its National Partnerships for AI initiative.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: This creates a formal channel for government-linked research and education institutions to use advanced AI models for scientific and public-sector applications.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Google announced a partnership with Karmayogi Bharat, which implements Mission Karmayogi, India’s civil service training programme.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Google Cloud will provide infrastructure support for the iGOT Karmayogi platform, the Government of India’s digital learning and capacity-building portal for civil servants that delivers structured online training and competency-based professional development across ministries and departments.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Google also announced the establishment of a Google Center for Climate Technology in collaboration with the Office of the Principal Scientific Advisor to the Government of India.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: OpenAI announced a series of enterprise and platform partnerships across IT services, streaming, travel, fintech and education. The company also confirmed plans to open offices in Mumbai and Bengaluru, expanding beyond its current presence in New Delhi.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: OpenAI announced that Tata Consultancy Services will integrate ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex into internal systems to build industry-specific AI solutions for enterprise clients.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Separately, OpenAI said it will work with TCS’s data centre subsidiary, HyperVault, to build AI data centre infrastructure as part of its Stargate initiative. The project is expected to begin at 100 MW and scale toward 1 GW capacity, aligned with OpenAI’s broader global infrastructure expansion plans.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: JioHotstar will integrate ChatGPT-powered conversational search into its platform. Instead of keyword-based discovery, users will be able to describe what they want to watch in natural language. The feature is expected to work across both live and on-demand formats.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Eternal, the parent company of Zomato, said it will deploy OpenAI’s Enterprise APIs across Blinkit, District and Hyperpure. The integration is expected to support operational workflows, search, discovery and decision-support systems.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: MakeMyTrip is integrating OpenAI APIs to power its AI travel assistant, Myra. The aim is to convert conversational queries directly into bookings for flights, hotels and other services. The company recently disclosed that its chatbot assistant handles more than 50,000 conversions per day, up from roughly 25,000 earlier.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: IXIGO announced it will integrate agentic AI across internal systems and consumer platforms, including ConfirmKit and AbhiBus, to support booking and customer workflows.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Pine Labs will use OpenAI’s APIs to build what it describes as an agentic stack for financial transactions. The company said it intends to open parts of this stack to third-party developers, enabling AI-enabled fintech applications within regulated environments.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Through its partnership with the Tata Group, OpenAI also said it will introduce AI-related professional certifications, with TCS participating as the first non-US organisation in its certification programme.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Anthropic formally opened its Bengaluru office and announced partnerships spanning enterprise, public infrastructure, education and agriculture. The company said India is its second-largest market for Claude.ai, with nearly 50% of usage tied to building applications, modernising systems and shipping production software.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: A central announcement was Anthropic’s collaboration with Infosys to deploy AI agents across telecommunications, financial services, manufacturing and software development.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: The partnership integrates Claude models and Claude Code with Infosys Topaz, Infosys’ generative and agentic AI platform. Infosys will embed Claude into enterprise workflows in compliance-sensitive sectors, beginning with telecommunications through a dedicated Center of Excellence.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: The focus is on agentic AI systems that execute multi-step tasks rather than respond to single prompts. Use cases include:

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: In telecommunications, the companies said agents will modernise network operations and customer lifecycle management. In financial services, the emphasis is compliance automation and risk assessment. In manufacturing, the focus is accelerating product design and simulation.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: The collaboration positions Anthropic’s India strategy around enterprise deployment in regulated sectors rather than consumer applications.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Anthropic detailed efforts to improve model performance across major Indian languages. Over the past six months, the company curated higher-quality training data in 10 widely spoken languages, including Hindi, Bengali, Marathi, Tamil, Telugu, Gujarati and Urdu.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: It is working with Karya and the Collective Intelligence Project to build domain-specific evaluation benchmarks across agriculture and law, in partnership with organisations such as Digital Green and Adalat AI. These evaluations are expected to be made publicly available.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Anthropic highlighted its Model Context Protocol (MCP), an open standard for connecting AI systems to external data sources, which has been donated to the Linux Foundation.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: India’s Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation, with support from Bharat Digital, has launched an official government MCP server that allows AI systems to query national statistical datasets. Swiggy is also using MCP to enable ordering workflows through Claude.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Anthropic is supporting Adalat AI in launching a WhatsApp-based judicial helpline that uses Claude to provide case updates, translation and document summarisation in Indian languages.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Educational and instructional tasks account for 12% of Claude usage in India. Anthropic is partnering with Pratham on its Anytime Testing Machine, currently piloted across 20 schools and adapted for learners in the Second Chance programme.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: The company is also collaborating with Central Square Foundation to support AI-enabled tutoring and teacher tools aimed at underserved communities.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: In nonprofit and healthcare settings, Claude Code and Cowork are being deployed with organisations such as Noora Health and Intelehealth to support documentation and operational workflows.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Microsoft said at the summit that it is on pace to invest $50 billion in AI across countries in the Global South by the end of the decade.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: The company described this as part of its broader strategy to expand AI infrastructure, skilling programmes and multilingual AI capabilities in emerging markets. Microsoft did not announce India-specific enterprise partnerships at the summit but framed its participation around long-term investment and diffusion across the Global South.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Jio showcased a set of AI applications that it framed as components of a broader “AI stack,” combining sector-focused tools with planned investments in domestic compute and network infrastructure. Rather than announcing a single flagship model, the company presented AI as an embedded layer across multiple verticals.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Jio Arogya AI is designed as a primary healthcare screening system that can assess symptoms in local languages and generate basic referral recommendations. The tool appears intended for integration into frontline or community health workflows, suggesting use cases in clinics or public health settings rather than as a direct-to-consumer medical application.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Jio AI Homes operates as an AI orchestration layer for connected devices. It is meant to coordinate and automate smart home functions across IoT systems, positioning AI as a control interface within Jio’s broader connectivity ecosystem. The emphasis was on integration with existing digital infrastructure rather than launching a standalone hardware product.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Jio Creator AI remains under development. The company described it as a tool to generate advertisements and content assets for creators and small businesses. Our reporters at the summit examined the system as part of Jio’s showcase. It was presented as a future-facing capability and has not yet been deployed.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: The Jio Shiksha Classroom Interface is also in development. It is designed to support structured classroom environments by assisting teachers with lesson delivery and enabling personalised student engagement. Jio positioned it as an institutional support tool for schools rather than a standalone AI tutoring app. It remains an early-stage initiative.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Jio also showcased JioFrames, AI-enabled smart glasses powered by Jio’s Voice AI, positioning them as a hands-free consumer interface for its broader AI ecosystem. The device supports real-time Indian language translation, voice assistance, hands-free calls, music streaming, photo and video capture, and live streaming. With JioFrames, the company extends its AI stack from telecom networks and enterprise systems into wearable hardware, embedding AI directly into everyday user interaction.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Alongside these applications, Jio outlined a “Nation-First AI Stack,” signalling plans to couple sectoral AI systems with domestic cloud, network, and compute investments. The framing suggests an effort to integrate AI services with Jio’s telecom infrastructure rather than operate purely as a model provider.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Airtel used the summit to showcase how it is embedding AI across its telecom networks, enterprise services and data centre infrastructure, rather than launching a standalone consumer AI product.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Airtel demonstrated its AI-driven fraud detection system, which operates at the network layer. The system analyses traffic patterns and behavioural signals in real time to identify spam calls, scam attempts and malicious links. By integrating AI directly into telecom infrastructure, the company aims to filter suspicious activity before it reaches end users.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Beyond consumer spam detection, Airtel highlighted AI-driven enterprise security tools. These include verified business name display features designed to reduce impersonation risks, telecom network APIs intended to support secure digital services and AI-based threat detection aligned with zero-trust security models. The focus was on using AI to strengthen trust within enterprise communication systems.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Airtel also outlined investments in AI-ready cloud services and the expansion of its Nxtra data centre network. The company described its infrastructure as optimised to support AI workloads and real-time analytics, while maintaining domestic data residency. It further indicated that AI is being used internally to manage network efficiency and resilience across its distributed data centres.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Overall, Airtel positioned AI as embedded digital infrastructure spanning telecom networks, enterprise security and cloud systems, rather than as a standalone application layer.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Unlike general-purpose LLMs, NPCI designed FiMI to operate within high-scale, high-trust environments such as Unified Payments Interface and other national payment rails.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: FiMI reflects vertically integrated AI embedded within the national financial infrastructure rather than layered as an external chatbot interface.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: PhonePe launched an AI-powered natural language search feature built on Microsoft Foundry, Microsoft’s enterprise AI development platform that allows companies to build and deploy generative AI applications using large language models and related tools.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Instead of navigating through menu-based options, users can type or speak instructions such as “Pay Hemanth 20 rupees” or “Recharge FASTag.” The system interprets the intent behind the request and routes the user directly to the relevant transaction or service page.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: This replaces traditional menu navigation with intent-based routing, where an AI layer translates conversational commands into specific in-app actions.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: By integrating natural language understanding into transaction workflows, PhonePe is shifting from a purely transactional interface toward a conversational layer embedded within its payments infrastructure.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Razorpay and the NPCI announced a partnership to enable “Agentic Payments” on Claude. The system is currently in pilot with a closed user group.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: The initiative integrates secure payment capabilities directly into AI-led conversations, allowing users to complete transactions within the chat interface without switching to separate apps or checkout pages.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Through the integration, users can place orders for food, groceries and quick commerce services from platforms such as Zomato, Swiggy and Zepto directly via Claude.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Instead of redirecting users to external payment gateways, the AI agent can complete transactions within the conversational flow after confirming intent.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: The system operates on UPI Reserve Pay, which allows users to provide one-time, consent-based authorization with predefined spending limits for a merchant.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Within those limits, the AI agent can initiate payments without repeated PIN or OTP prompts. Users retain the ability to revoke consent and monitor transactions.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: The integration reflects a shift from AI-assisted discovery toward AI-executed commerce. Rather than stopping at product search or comparison, conversational AI platforms are moving toward end-to-end transaction completion within India’s UPI-enabled ecosystem.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Google demonstrated a Gemini-powered AI cricket coach that uses conversational prompts to guide batting, bowling and fielding techniques. Instead of offering static tutorials, the system allows users to ask questions and receive structured responses in a back-and-forth format, simulating an interactive coaching session.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: The demonstration aligns with Google’s broader partnership with the International Cricket Council for the ICC Men’s T20 World Cup 2026. Under that arrangement, Gemini has been designated as the tournament’s Official AI Fan Companion, and Google Pixel as the Official Smartphone.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: The Indian Army showcased a set of deployable indigenous AI systems designed for operational readiness, surveillance and decision support in mission environments. Unlike experimental demonstrations, the Army presented these as field-ready tools integrated into defence workflows.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Prakshepan is described as an AI-based military climatology and disaster prediction system. It analyses environmental and atmospheric data to support operational planning, particularly in regions vulnerable to extreme weather or terrain disruptions.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: XFace functions as a facial recognition platform intended for rapid identity verification in controlled environments. It is positioned for use in security-sensitive deployments where quick authentication is required.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Nabhdrishti is a telemetry and aerial monitoring system that processes real-time data feeds to support surveillance and situational tracking in mission zones.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: The Army also demonstrated AI-enabled fatigue monitoring tools designed to assess driver alertness levels, aiming to reduce accidents and operational risks during long deployments.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: AI-in-a-Box is presented as a portable AI toolkit that can be deployed in the field. It allows AI capabilities to operate in constrained or remote environments without reliance on continuous connectivity.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: SAM-UN is a situational awareness module built to integrate multiple data inputs into a unified operational view, assisting commanders in mission planning and execution.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Together, these systems reflect a defence-focused AI stack aimed at embedding machine intelligence directly into field operations rather than treating AI as a standalone experimental layer.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Wadhwani AI showcased an early tuberculosis detection system designed to assist frontline health workers in screening for pulmonary TB. The system analyses cough audio patterns along with symptom inputs through a smartphone-based interface to identify individuals who may require further clinical testing.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Rather than functioning as a diagnostic replacement, the tool is positioned as a screening aid intended to support early-stage detection and referral decisions in resource-constrained settings.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: SATHEE, developed by the Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur, was presented in its upgraded AI-enabled form at the summit. The platform supports competitive exam preparation across national entrance tests such as JEE, NEET, CUET and CLAT.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: The system incorporates conversational tutoring, adaptive learning tools and multilingual resources, allowing students to interact with content and resolve doubts through AI-assisted interfaces. Unlike standalone chatbot experiments, SATHEE is positioned as a structured academic support platform integrated into exam preparation workflows.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Amul launched Sarlaben, an artificial intelligence assistant designed for its network of 36 lakh milk producers managing nearly 3 crore cattle. The system is currently being rolled out within Amul’s cooperative network in Gujarat.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: Sarlaben is integrated with Amul’s Automatic Milk Collection System and Pashudhan applications, allowing it to draw on operational and livestock-level data across the cooperative database. It provides personalised guidance on cattle health, vaccination schedules, medical treatment, feeding practices and breeding management. The assistant also shares information on government schemes and subsidies available to dairy farmers.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: The tool runs on Amul’s existing digital infrastructure, which processes over 200 crore milk procurement transactions annually. Farmers can access it through the Amul Farmer Mobile Application or via phone calls, extending availability beyond smartphone-only users.

title: Here’s What Companies Unveiled at India AI Impact Summit 2026
paragraph: The announcements mark only the first stage. The real test will lie in execution, interoperability and long-term integration across sectors.

title: ‘Sounds like spam, how?’: Netizens sceptical after man claims AI use to apply to 1,000 jobs, get 50 calls, debate ethics
paragraph: Netizens over at Reddit have reacted with scepticism and debate over ethics after a man posted claiming that he used artificial intelligence (AI) to send 1,000 job application while sleeping and landed 50 job interviews over a period of one month.

title: ‘Sounds like spam, how?’: Netizens sceptical after man claims AI use to apply to 1,000 jobs, get 50 calls, debate ethics
paragraph: Posting on subreddit ‘Get Employed’, the original poster (OP), who has at time of writing this story on January 12, deleted his account. He claimed that he used AI to “automatically apply for 1000 jobs - and I got 50 interviews!”

title: ‘Sounds like spam, how?’: Netizens sceptical after man claims AI use to apply to 1,000 jobs, get 50 calls, debate ethics
paragraph: The OP claimed he created an AI bot that analysed candidate information, job descriptions and “generates unique CVs and cover letters for each job”. He added, “And all of this while I was sleeping! In just one month, this method helped me secure around 50 interviews. The tailored CVs and cover letters, customized based on each job description, made a significant difference.”

title: ‘Sounds like spam, how?’: Netizens sceptical after man claims AI use to apply to 1,000 jobs, get 50 calls, debate ethics
paragraph: Netizens React With Scepticism, Debate Ethics Netizens however were not buying the tall claims. One quipped: “This sounds like some AI bot spamming Reddit.” (sic) and another checked out OP's link and warned other users saying, “WARNING. As of 2024-01-08, the code will not work because it's half-baked!”

title: ‘Sounds like spam, how?’: Netizens sceptical after man claims AI use to apply to 1,000 jobs, get 50 calls, debate ethics
paragraph: Some others were cautions, adding their input: “Careful. Companies are starting to add a “no AI” rule for resumes/applications. We already do and I even suspect AI was used, your resume goes straight in the trash.” (sic) and OP in comments wrote that the tool is for “education”, saying, “I do not recommend the use of this bot for obvious ethical reasons. The bot was created for educational and informative purposes only and out of my own curiosity. Nonetheless, I wanted to share the odd results obtained to highlight the power of artificial intelligence! However, I must also say that to be fair, there are many very expensive services that claim to offer the same functionality, probably much less than my bot!” (sic)

title: ‘Sounds like spam, how?’: Netizens sceptical after man claims AI use to apply to 1,000 jobs, get 50 calls, debate ethics
paragraph: Not all agreed however, “Unethical? I don’t know. Companies use AI to read applications/ resumes so it’s pretty much bot talking to bot. If it gets me the job in the 21st century then like the old 20th century saying goes, all's fair in love and war,” said one user.

title: A roadmap for responsible AI
paragraph: Widespread AI use introduces risks such as bias, safety, transparency, and security concerns. With evolving regulations like the EU AI Act, implementing a comprehensive risk management framework is essential.

title: A roadmap for responsible AI
paragraph: A responsible AI framework operationalises guidelines, oversight, and technical safeguards to optimise outcomes. Enterprises should adopt a three-pronged approach that includes monitoring risks, enhancing defences and development, and governing control and risk management.

title: A roadmap for responsible AI
paragraph: Codified AI policies: Enterprises must establish clear AI policies covering procurement, development, deployment, grievance redress, usage, and crisis management. These accessible policies should define stakeholder obligations and adherence levels, forming the basis of AI governance.

title: A roadmap for responsible AI
paragraph: Metrics driven and streamlined governance: Enterprises need to define metrics in privacy, explainability, and security to track responsible AI usage. Automating compliance checks accelerates low-risk approvals.

title: A roadmap for responsible AI
paragraph: The responsible AI office: Enterprises require a dedicated team with mandates to align ethical considerations with business objectives, avoiding conflicts of interest.

title: A roadmap for responsible AI
paragraph: Responsible by design focuses on integrating ethical principles, fairness, transparency, accountability, and regulatory compliance across the AI lifecycle, from design to deployment. Key aspects of this approach include:

title: A roadmap for responsible AI
paragraph: Impact and risk assessment: Enterprises must leverage automated risk assessment to identify and categorise use-cases for different risks, applying additional gating criteria and design consideration during development stage.

title: A roadmap for responsible AI
paragraph: Red teaming: This is central to an AI testing strategy. Enterprises should have teams skilled in adversarial testing processes to expose hidden vulnerabilities in models and use cases.

title: A roadmap for responsible AI
paragraph: Responsible AI toolkit: Enterprises need specialised tools with APIs for developers to automate and streamline responsible design and development, integrating considerations like fairness, explainability, and privacy across stages.

title: A roadmap for responsible AI
paragraph: Legal guardrails: Organisations need to apply legal safeguards, such as contractual clauses, to minimise risks from third parties, review procurement and usage policies legally.

title: A roadmap for responsible AI
paragraph: Aligning Gen AI with ethical standards is challenging due to the complex AI supply chain and industry-specific risks. To mitigate this, real-time guardrails that monitor AI inputs and outputs for threats are effective. Investing in responsible AI is crucial as consumer demands for AI safety grow.

title: The future of consulting in the age of Generative AI
paragraph: In a world of constant technological evolution, industries must adapt or risk becoming obsolete. Consulting, a field rooted in providing expert advice, faces a pivotal moment of transformation as Generative Artificial Intelligence (Generative AI) continues to redefine the boundaries of what is possible. This paradigm shift presents both challenges and opportunities for the consulting industry, as it ushers in a new era of unprecedented capabilities and expectations.

title: The future of consulting in the age of Generative AI
paragraph: Generative AI, like OpenAI’s GPT-3, has revolutionized the way we interact with technology, offering machines the ability to generate human-like text, images, and more. Its applications span various sectors, from content creation to healthcare, finance, and customer service. The consulting industry, traditionally reliant on human expertise, stands to be significantly impacted by this technological wave.

title: The future of consulting in the age of Generative AI
paragraph: Consultants have long been revered for their deep knowledge and experience in specific domains. Generative AI can augment this expertise by rapidly processing vast datasets, extracting insights, and providing data-driven recommendations. Consultants will have access to a powerful ally, one capable of analyzing and synthesizing information at an unprecedented scale. This partnership between humans and AI can help consultants make more informed decisions, backed by comprehensive data.

title: The future of consulting in the age of Generative AI
paragraph: Generative AI’s ability to process and interpret large datasets can streamline the consulting process. Consultants can leverage AI-driven analytics tools to gain insights that were previously difficult or time-consuming to uncover. This will not only enhance the accuracy and reliability of recommendations but also save valuable time.

title: The future of consulting in the age of Generative AI
paragraph: With Generative AI’s ability to generate personalized content and solutions, consultants can offer highly tailored recommendations to clients. This level of customization can lead to more effective strategies and improved client satisfaction. AI-powered chatbots and virtual assistants can also provide immediate responses to client queries, enhancing the overall client experience.

title: The future of consulting in the age of Generative AI
paragraph: Generative AI can serve as a powerful tool for continuous learning and skill enhancement within the consulting industry. It can be used for knowledge transfer, training, and skill development, allowing consultants to stay updated with the latest industry trends and best practices.

title: The future of consulting in the age of Generative AI
paragraph: The rise of Generative AI will likely disrupt traditional consulting business models. Some roles may become automated, requiring consultants to adapt and acquire new skills. However, new opportunities for specialization in AI consulting, AI ethics, and AI integration will emerge.

title: The future of consulting in the age of Generative AI
paragraph: Consultants will need to collaborate closely with AI specialists, data scientists, and engineers to harness the full potential of Generative AI. Building multidisciplinary teams will be essential for creating innovative solutions and services that blend human expertise with AI capabilities.

title: The future of consulting in the age of Generative AI
paragraph: The future of consulting in the age of Generative AI is one of transformation, collaboration, and opportunity. While Generative AI has the potential to streamline processes, enhance expertise, and offer unprecedented insights, it also raises important ethical questions that require careful consideration. The successful consulting firms of the future will be those that adapt to this new landscape, harness the power of AI, and continue to provide valuable, human-driven expertise while leveraging the capabilities of Generative AI to the fullest. It’s an exciting era for the consulting industry, one that promises to redefine the way we think about expertise and problem-solving.

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: Artificial Intelligence has quietly entered almost every corner of modern life — from the moment Google Maps reroutes your drive to when ChatGPT helps you draft an email or when your phone camera adjusts light automatically.

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: But behind the convenience lies a hidden cost — one we rarely see: the vast amounts of energy, water, and minerals that keep this “intelligence” alive.

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: Every query, every image generation, and every chatbot reply travels through massive data centres that consume electricity equivalent to small towns.

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is transforming nearly every sector. This transformation brings environmental benefits – such as improved climate forecasting and optimized energy use – but also has a substantial ecological footprint through electricity consumption, water use and material demands. Understanding both sides of AI’s environmental ledger is essential for policymakers and technology developers aiming for sustainable AI.

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: > High electricity demand: Generative AI models have billions of parameters and require huge computational power. MIT researchers noted that training clusters for generative AI can draw seven–eight times more energy than typical computing workloads . Consequently, North‑American data‑centre capacity jumped from 2688 MW in 2022 to 5341 MW in 2023, and global data‑centre electricity consumption reached 460 TWh in 2022; by 2026 it is expected to reach 1050 TWh, which would rank data centres fifth among all electricity consumers worldwide .

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: > Model training emissions: Training large language models consumes vast amounts of power. The training of OpenAI’s GPT‑3 was estimated to use 1287 MWh of electricity, producing = 552 t of CO₂ , while a 2019 study found that training a large natural‑language model can emit 626 000 lbs (~284 t) CO₂e, roughly five times the lifetime emissions of an average U.S. car .

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: > Inference energy: Once deployed, AI models still consume energy every time a user queries them. Researchers estimate that ChatGPT queries use about five times more electricity than a simple web search . A 2024 International Energy Agency analysis suggests that if ChatGPT replaced 9 billion daily Google searches, it would require nearly 10 TWh annually .

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: > Air pollution and health costs: A UC Riverside/Caltech study projects that additional electricity demand from AI could lead to ≈1 300 premature deaths per year in the U.S. by 2030 due to air pollution and impose ≈US$20 billion in annual health costs . The study warns that electricity for AI often comes from fossil‑fuel plants and backup diesel generators and that the resulting pollution disproportionately affects communities near data centres .

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: > Data centres use large volumes of water for cooling, MIT’s analysis notes that roughly two litres of water are needed for every kilowatt‑hour of electricity consumed . UNEP warns that AI‑driven infrastructure could soon consume six times more water than Denmark, straining local ecosystems .

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: > Water consumption is not only direct; high‑performance chips also require water during semiconductor fabrication, compounding AI’s water footprint .

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: > Rare‑earth materials: AI hardware (GPU chips, accelerators) relies on energy‑intensive manufacturing and rare earth minerals. Mining for these elements involves toxic chemicals and can damage ecosystems .

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: > Electronic waste (e‑waste): Data‑centre servers are replaced every few years. UNEP notes that constructing a 2 kg computer requires ≈800 kg of raw materials, and discarded servers contribute to e‑waste containing mercury and lead .

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: Despite its footprint, AI is an indispensable tool for environmental protection when applied thoughtfully. The sections below highlight specific areas where AI benefits the environment.

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: > Flood forecasting: Google’s Flood Hub uses AI to combine historical river levels, terrain data and hydrological models. It can forecast riverine floods up to seven days in advance and has expanded coverage to 80 countries, reaching 460 million people . The model improved reliability in Africa and Asia to match European standards.

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: > Weather forecasting: AI‑enhanced systems like DeepMind’s GraphCast analyze decades of atmospheric data to predict weather events. Reuters reports that such models provide highly specific forecasts — for instance, predicting heavy rain in a downtown street or mountainous valley — and outperform many traditional physics‑based models . These forecasts help communities prepare for extreme weather and optimize resource use.

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: > Wildfire and storm prediction: Machine‑learning algorithms detect patterns in satellite imagery and sensor data to identify potential wildfires and severe storms, enabling early evacuation and targeted interventions (general concept derived from Reuters and Google flood‑forecasting sources).

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: > Data‑centre efficiency: DeepMind’s AI‑driven cooling control at Google’s data centres achieved a 40 % reduction in cooling energy use, yielding a 15 % reduction in overall power‑usage effectiveness (PUE) overhead . The system continuously adjusts cooling parameters in real time, setting new energy‑efficiency benchmarks.

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: > Building energy management: AI‑powered HVAC systems learn usage patterns and adjust heating and cooling accordingly. A Manhattan office building (45 Broadway) equipped with BrainBox AI software cut HVAC energy consumption by ≈15.8 % over 11 months, saving US$42 000 and reducing 37 metric tons of CO₂ equivalent. A study by Time estimated that integrating AI into HVAC systems could reduce energy use and carbon emissions by 8–19 %.

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: > Smart grids and renewable integration: AI algorithms balance electricity supply and demand, forecast renewable generation and manage battery storage. A PwC/Microsoft/Oxford University study described by Triple Pundit suggests that if AI drives energy‑efficiency gains even one‑tenth as fast as its adoption, the net impact of AI on global energy use could become neutral or positive. The same report notes that digital energy tools could save companies US$2 trillion per year by 2030 through optimized energy management .

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: > Vehicle‑to‑grid integration: Cornell University’s 2025 project funded by the Bezos Earth Fund is developing AI‑driven tools to coordinate charging and discharging of millions of electric vehicles (EVs) so they act as mobile grid batteries. The project seeks to decarbonize the power and transport sectors by using idle EVs (parked ≈95 % of the time) as a distributed energy reservoir, smoothing intermittent renewable supply and strengthening grid resilience.

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: AI‑driven precision farming shows significant potential to reduce environmental impacts of agriculture while improving yields:

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: > Water and fertilizer efficiency: Smart irrigation systems powered by AI have reduced water use by up to 40 %. Targeted fertilizer recommendations improve soil health and cut costs by 20–35 %.

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: > Reduced pesticides and herbicides: Precision spraying guided by AI reduces pesticide use by 30–50 %. John Deere’s “See & Spray” system decreased herbicide application by up to 90 %, improved productivity by 20 % and lowered costs by 15 % on large farms in the U.S. and Europe.

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: > Disease and pest management: AI models can detect crop infections with up to 98 % accuracy, allowing early intervention and reducing pesticide use by 40 % . IBM Watson’s system predicted fungal disease outbreaks in Brazilian soybean farms, reducing infections by 40 %.

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: > Yield improvement: AI‑integrated unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) increased crop yields by 15–30 % through continuous monitoring ; predictive analytics improved yield forecasts with 95 % accuracy, lowering farmer losses by 20–35 % . Indian startup Fasal improved grape yields by 20 % while cutting water use by 50 %, and CropIn increased wheat yields by 25 % and reduced post‑harvest losses.

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: > Methane and pollution detection: AI systems can analyze satellite and sensor data to locate methane leaks, illegal deforestation or fishing, and pollution sources. UNEP highlighted AI’s potential to detect methane emissions, map dredging patterns and monitor biodiversity.

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: > Wildlife conservation: Computer vision models identify species from camera‑trap images, enabling conservationists to monitor endangered animals and combat poaching without invasive methods.

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: Energy‑intensive data‑centre expansion: Generative AI’s appetite for computation drives rapid construction of data centres. MIT researchers warn that the pace of data‑centre build‑out means most of the necessary electricity will come from fossil‑fuel power plants .

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: Large data centres often cluster near rivers or communities with scarce water resources. UNEP warns that AI infrastructure could soon consume more water than some countries and that this water is often withdrawn from local supplies, harming aquatic ecosystems and competing with agriculture. Communities near data‑centre clusters may experience declining groundwater levels and increased water prices.

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: Demand for GPUs and accelerators accelerates mining of rare‑earth elements and cobalt. Extraction processes use toxic chemicals and produce hazardous waste . Short hardware lifecycles contribute to e‑waste; discarded servers contain mercury and lead and can leach into soil and water if not properly recycled.

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: The UC Riverside/Caltech study warns that meeting AI’s electricity demand with fossil‑fuel plants could cause ≈1 300 premature deaths per year and about US$20 billion in annual health costs by 2030 . Pollution from AI‑powered data centres disproportionately affects low‑income communities located near power plants and backup diesel generators .

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: While AI improves agricultural productivity, high costs of precision equipment (US$50 000–500 000) and subscription fees exclude smallholder farmers . Limited digital literacy and language barriers also hinder adoption . AI‑driven farm machinery reduces labour demand, potentially displacing workers . Concentration of agricultural data by large corporations raises concerns about data privacy, monopolization and bias .

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: Energy‑efficient algorithms and hardware: Researchers and developers should design models with fewer parameters, employ pruning and quantization techniques, and prefer efficient architectures. Hardware innovations such as Google’s Ironwood TPU (designed for AI inference) reportedly doubled energy efficiency compared with its predecessor .

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: Renewable and low‑carbon power: Tech companies can power data centres with renewable energy and explore emerging low‑carbon sources like small modular reactors (SMRs). Several hyperscalers have committed to investing in nuclear projects, while some (e.g., Apple) run data centres on 100 % renewable energy. Building data‑centre structures from low‑carbon materials, such as wood, can further reduce embodied emissions.

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: Water‑smart cooling: Adopt technologies such as immersion cooling, heat‑pump systems and situating data centres in cooler climates to reduce water usage. Reusing waste heat for district heating can improve overall energy efficiency.

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: E‑waste management and circular design: Extend hardware lifespans through modular upgrades and design servers for disassembly and recycling. Encourage manufacturers to disclose material footprints and implement producer‑responsibility schemes.

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: Transparency and reporting: Regulators should require companies to disclose AI‑related energy consumption, water use, and emissions. UNEP advocates for standardized measurement and reporting frameworks , enabling policymakers to assess AI’s environmental impact accurately.

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: Equitable access and farmer support: In agriculture, governments can subsidize AI tools for smallholder farmers and invest in training programmes to improve digital literacy. Developing open‑source AI platforms and mobile‑friendly applications can broaden access .

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: AI for environmental good: Continue developing AI applications that reduce emissions elsewhere — such as smart grid management, optimized building control, precision agriculture and wildlife monitoring — so that efficiency gains can offset AI’s own footprint .

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: “AI’s environmental impact is dual‑edged. On one hand, the computational demands of large models and data centres drive up electricity consumption, water use, carbon emissions, and resource extraction. Without careful planning, AI could undermine climate goals and exacerbate pollution, inequity and resource scarcity. On the other hand, AI offers powerful tools for environmental stewardship: forecasting floods and extreme weather, optimizing energy systems and integrating renewables, improving building efficiency, conserving water and reducing chemical use in agriculture, and monitoring biodiversity. The challenge is to align AI development with sustainability, ensuring that efficiency gains and environmental monitoring capabilities outweigh the footprint of AI itself. Policies promoting renewable energy, transparent reporting, equitable access, and responsible innovation are essential to harness AI as a force for environmental good.”

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: Before we wrap up, let’s address a few questions that naturally arise when we talk about AI and the environment — questions that help us think more deeply about how we use technology today.

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: Ans: AI contributes to climate change primarily through energy consumption. Training large AI models like GPT-3 can use over 1,200 MWh of electricity, producing hundreds of tons of CO₂ emissions. Even after deployment, every AI query consumes energy — about five times more than a normal web search — adding to the overall carbon footprint.

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: Ans: Data centres use water to cool high-performance servers that power AI models. On average, two litres of water are required for every kWh of electricity consumed. When millions of AI computations run daily, this demand strains local water resources — especially in regions already facing water scarcity.

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: Ans: Yes — if innovation aligns with sustainability. Researchers are developing energy-efficient algorithms, companies are shifting to renewable-powered data centres, and policymakers are exploring reporting frameworks for emissions and water usage. The goal isn’t to limit AI’s growth but to ensure it evolves as a climate-conscious technology.

title: Dual Impact Of AI On The Environment: Powering Progress, Pressuring The Planet?
paragraph: Shikha Kumari is an SEO, AEO, LEO — Organic Growth Expert with 7+ years of experience helping brands stay discoverable across platforms.

title: Centre tightens AI content rules, slashes takedown window to 3 hours
paragraph: The Centre on Tuesday sharply tightened compliance norms for social media platforms and online intermediaries, mandating the takedown of unlawful AI-generated and synthetic content within just three hours of being flagged, a steep cut from the earlier 36-hour window.

title: Centre tightens AI content rules, slashes takedown window to 3 hours
paragraph: In a gazette notification, the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) notified the Information Technology (Intermediary Guidelines and Digital Media Ethics Code) Amendment Rules, 2026, which for the first time lay down detailed obligations for identifying, labelling and acting against AI-generated, manipulated and deepfake content. The rules, which come into force on February 20, are expected to significantly raise compliance costs and legal exposure for global platforms such as Meta-owned Facebook and Instagram, YouTube and X.

title: Centre tightens AI content rules, slashes takedown window to 3 hours
paragraph: Under the amended framework, intermediaries are required to act expeditiously once they become aware of content classified as synthetically generated information (SGI), including content created, modified or altered using AI tools. Such material must be taken down within three hours of notification. The rules also make mandatory the clear and prominent labelling of AI-generated content, with platforms required to embed permanent metadata or identifiers wherever technically feasible, and to ensure that such labels cannot be removed or suppressed.

title: Centre tightens AI content rules, slashes takedown window to 3 hours
paragraph: The move underscores the government’s increasingly interventionist stance on AI governance at a time when the technology is rapidly scaling across sectors. Significantly, the amendments have been notified just days before India hosts the AI Impact Summit from February 16 to 20, which will bring together global technology leaders including Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang, Google CEO Sundar Pichai, Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei and Gates Foundation chair Bill Gates to discuss responsible AI deployment and global governance standards.

title: German broadcaster recalls correspondent over AI-generated images
paragraph: German public broadcaster ZDF on Friday recalled a New York correspondent after AI-generated images were screened during a news report on ICE immigration raids in the United States.

title: German broadcaster recalls correspondent over AI-generated images
paragraph: ZDF said its journalist Nicola Albrecht, 50, used video taken from the internet in a report on children terrified by US Immigrations and Customs Enforcement operations.

title: German broadcaster recalls correspondent over AI-generated images
paragraph: "The damage caused by disregarding journalistic rules is considerable," ZDF editor-in-chief Bettina Schausten said in a statement. "At its core, this is about the credibility of our reporting."

title: German broadcaster recalls correspondent over AI-generated images
paragraph: Albrecht's original report broadcast on February 13 was accurate, ZDF said, but an updated version broadcast on the February 15 edition of the flagship nightly news programme contained the two misleading clips.

title: German broadcaster recalls correspondent over AI-generated images
paragraph: Presenter Dunja Hayali had introduced the segment saying the Trump administration's immigration raids had created "a climate of fear that doesn't even stop at children".

title: German broadcaster recalls correspondent over AI-generated images
paragraph: One clip could be seen to feature the watermark of Sora, OpenAI's platform that generates short video clips based on prompts.

title: German broadcaster recalls correspondent over AI-generated images
paragraph: "The AI-generated material should not have been used without journalistic justification and without being categorised according to ZDF's internal rules for the use of AI-generated material," the broadcaster said.

title: German broadcaster recalls correspondent over AI-generated images
paragraph: Publications including Wired and Business Insider in August withdrew features purportedly written by a freelance journalist following concerns they were in fact written using generative artificial intelligence.

title: German broadcaster recalls correspondent over AI-generated images
paragraph: In January, AFP factcheckers found that an image carried by ZDF purporting to show former Venezuelan president Nicolas Maduro after his capture by US soldiers was AI-generated.

title: What is a chatbot? The rise, risks, and impact of generative AI
paragraph: The gist The mass adoption of generative AI chatbots like ChatGPT is driving market growth for various professional and consumer uses Current AI chatbots excel at human-like conversation and complex tasks, but they present risks like hallucinations and potential harm. Chatbots continue to evolve but are here to stay. This makes it critical for IT leaders to understand the benefits and risks.

title: What is a chatbot? The rise, risks, and impact of generative AI
paragraph: An explosion of chatbots brought on by rapid advancements in generative AI over the last few years has led to mass adoption of the tools both by enterprises and consumers for a variety of use cases.

title: What is a chatbot? The rise, risks, and impact of generative AI
paragraph: While the tools are lauded for their speed, comprehension of natural language and efficiency, in some instances, the digital tools have been blamed for dangerous suggestions leading to real harm.

title: What is a chatbot? The rise, risks, and impact of generative AI
paragraph: Still, there’s no sign of these technologies or their adoption slowing down. Market research company Mordor Intelligence estimates the global chatbot market to grow from a market size of $9.3 billion today to $29.07 billion by 2030.

title: Top 9 generative AI use cases for business
paragraph: Gen AI can help pharmaceutical companies predict drug interactions, repurpose existing drugs, and create personalized therapies based on a patient’s genetic makeup, according to MSRcosmos, a global IT services provider.

title: Top 9 generative AI use cases for business
paragraph: In early 2024, NVIDIA announced its AI-driven Clara computing platform targeting the healthcare industry and its BioNeMo, a gen AI platform for drug discovery.

title: Top 9 generative AI use cases for business
paragraph: Some biotech and pharma companies, including Johnson & Johnson, are promoting gen AI as the next big thing in drug discovery.

title: Top 9 generative AI use cases for business
paragraph: Several cybersecurity firms are using gen AI to enhance tools that look for suspicious or unusual behavior on a customer’s network and computing infrastructure. AI systems can also be used for advanced fraud detection that predict fraudulent activities with great accuracy by analyzing transaction patterns and user behaviors, says Jim Kaskade, CEO of Conversica, a provider of conversational automation solutions.

title: Top 9 generative AI use cases for business
paragraph: For example, Palo Alto Networks offers the Cortex XSIAM security operations platform, which combines the company’s expertise in ML models and its data store along with Google’s BigQuery enterprise data warehouse and its Gemini AI model. The goal is to alert security analysts to threats in real time, while the cybersecurity platform continually learns about new threats.

title: Top 9 generative AI use cases for business
paragraph: Generative AI is finding a sweet spot in enterprise business process augmentation. Here, companies are exploring the use of gen AI to provide efficiencies for business-critical workflows, often unique to their verticals.

title: Top 9 generative AI use cases for business
paragraph: For example, some firms in the finance and insurance industries are using gen AI to assist underwriters evaluating prospective clients. Credibly, a lending platform for small businesses, uses gen AI, paired with machine learning, to evaluate loan risk and to speed up the lending process, says Ryan Rosett, co-CEO and founder of the company.

title: Top 9 generative AI use cases for business
paragraph: “Gen AI at Credibly is being used to give our underwriters superpowers,” he says. “As a fintech lending company, our success depends on fast and accurate risk assessment of business owners seeking financing.”

title: Top 9 generative AI use cases for business
paragraph: Nearly all insurance carriers had adopted gen AI or were interested in it as of late 2023, according to a survey from EY. About 42% of insurers had already invested in gen AI, and about two-thirds expected a revenue boost of more than 10% through use of gen AI.

title: Top 9 generative AI use cases for business
paragraph: In the legal arena, legal information services giant LexisNexis is embracing generative AI to keep in front of what EVP and CTO Jeff Reihl sees as a disruptive threat in the company’s industry.

title: Top 9 generative AI use cases for business
