title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: If there’s one thing the Global Fintech Fest (GFF) 2025 proved, it’s that India’s fintech story can still surprise you even after six editions, a thousand panels, and an infinite number of AI jokes.

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: Over three charged days from October 7 to 9, Mumbai’s Jio World Centre turned into a buzzing fintech city of its own, where startup founders traded notes on agentic AI and FOMO diaries of the AI bus, central bankers swapped ideas on inclusion, and yes, Vijay Shekhar Sharma rode the Metro to make an entrance, while many got the shock of their lives seeing cab fares at the end of the day.

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: The theme this year, “Empowering Finance for a Better World Powered by AI,” couldn’t have been more fitting. Because from booths to boardrooms, from ministers to machine-learning demos, AI was everywhere in every conversation, presentation, and, sometimes, punchline.

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: You could sense it everywhere, right from the panel titles, booth conversations, even in casual banter over coffee. Every discussion circled back to some version of artificial intelligence: generative AI for lending, conversational bots in insurance, agentic AI in capital markets, or small language models for payments.

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: NVIDIA had a noticeably stronger presence at GFF this year. Along with NPCI, it hosted the Bharat AI Experience Zone, a space for firms to showcase working AI innovations through live demos.

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: If last year’s buzzword was fraud-tech or collections, this year it was unmistakably AI. “There’s a lot of FOMO,” one startup founder admitted. “Last year, the RBI wasn’t very keen on AI. This year, the sentiment has reversed seeing the global mood and the pressure to stay ahead, no one wants to miss this bus. Startups, even banks, are trying something, but it’s all very experimental right now.”

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: That summed up the tone well: excitement mixed with realism. Everyone wanted to ride the AI wave, but most admitted they were still learning how.

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: Voice AI in particular was quite prevalent with demos all around. For instance, DCB Bank’s new multilingual AudioBot built with Ringg AI. Bengaluru-based Gnani.ai grabbed headlines with the launch of its AI-generated Digital Human, a self-cloned avatar that could talk, blink, and emote in real time.

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: AI may have stolen the limelight, but UPI refused to fade quietly. RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra unveiled the much-anticipated Offline Digital Rupee, finally bringing tap-and-pay to digital cash. He also launched AI-based UPI Help, powered by NPCI’s small language model, IoT Payments for connected devices, and UPI Reserve Pay, allowing users to block and manage credit limits across apps.

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: The discussions around digital currency, though, weren’t all solemn policy talk. In one of the lighter moments, RBI Governor P. Vasudevan and Razorpay’s Harshil Mathur traded friendly jokes over how CBDCs might one day make payment aggregators redundant. The banter drew laughs across the room. Mathur quipped that programmability of money could “give colour to cash,” while Vasudevan good-humouredly replied that “some payment players may not like that.” It was a rare public peek at how regulators and fintechs can talk shop, share a laugh, and still debate the future of money.

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: There were enough moments of lightness to balance the jargon. Paytm’s Vijay Shekhar Sharma was in his element during his fireside chat being witty, self-aware, and disarmingly candid. “Haar ke jeetne wale ko baazigar kehte hain. Jeetke haarne wale ko aur wapas jeetne wale ko entrepreneur kehte hain,” he said to a laughing audience.

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: Elsewhere, old friends and fintech veterans Sahil Kini (now at RBIH) and Amrish Rao (Pine Labs) reunited on stage, drawing nostalgic smiles from the audience who remembered their earlier stint at Setu. The missing name, of course, was Sachin Bansal.

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: And if the mix of bankers and founders wasn’t eclectic enough, this year’s GFF had a touch of Bollywood too. Hrithik Roshan made an appearance as the brand ambassador for RUGR, the fintech startup he recently joined hands with. Meanwhile, Suniel Shetty turned up for the exclusive launch of Zrai Shield, an AI-powered fraud management system. It felt unusual and a little surreal to see film stars sharing the same stage where RBI officials had spoken just hours earlier. But that’s GFF now: part business, part buzz, and a little bit of showbiz.

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: The opening day saw the top policymakers set the tone. Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman launched the Foreign Currency Settlement System (FCSS), a mechanism for real-time forex settlement within the IFSC framework, while urging fintechs to balance innovation with risk and responsibility

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal struck an upbeat note, saying India had moved from being “a participant in global trade to a principal architect of the fintech world.” Calling trust “the only currency that never depreciates,” he urged Indian fintechs to “capture world markets” and make India the “centre of technology”

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: NPCI’s Ajay Kumar Choudhary spoke about the “promise and peril” of AI in finance, warning that as the industry leans on advanced chips, cloud systems, and large models, it risks over-dependence on a few players

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: Meanwhile, Nandan Nilekani reminded everyone that India had “achieved in nine years what would have taken 47” through its digital stack, as he outlined Finternet, a framework for tokenized and AI-enabled global digital infrastructure

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: Even SEBI Chair Tuhin Kanta Pandey and MoSPI Secretary Saurabh Garg had their say with Pandey highlighting India’s 134 million investors and faster settlements, and Garg describing a data system that’s becoming more open, AI-ready, and accountable

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: And then there was the UK Prime Minister’s special appearance on Day 3 — greeted by a glowing, decked-up Worli Sea Link, reminding everyone that GFF now commands a truly global stage.

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: Product launches came thick and fast. Razorpay, NPCI, and OpenAI unveiled Agentic Payments, allowing users to complete UPI transactions inside AI environments like ChatGPT. Cashfree announced a similar chat-embedded checkout experience, while Lenskart showed off a new feature for its upcoming smartglasses — QR-based payments with no phone or PIN required.

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: While government leaders and regulators set the policy tone at GFF 2025, venture capital presence at the venue itself was relatively muted. Except for a few firms like Z47, MUFG, Lightspeed, TVS Capital and Peak XV, most investors chose to keep a lower profile this year.

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: Several VCs were spotted in nearby hotels and cafés, meeting founders and hearing pitches away from the bustle of the Jio World Centre.

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: Many investors said this year’s fintech innovations felt “incremental rather than disruptive,” though they noted growing maturity in products and business models.

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: As one VC quipped off the record, “GFF is where we come to listen to the government in the morning and the startups in the evening, both tell us what the future will cost.”

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: As the sessions wound down each evening, the mood stayed light. Founders mingled with policymakers, some even turned DJs at the Startup Policy Forum mixer.

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: Music found its place too. The evenings at GFF weren’t all panel fatigue and PowerPoints. One of the highlights was a soulful sundowner by Shankar Mahadevan, hosted by Scapia, which had the crowd singing along after a long day of sessions.

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: The usual question floating across the pavilion wasn’t about valuation or funding; it was, “Bhai, tum ROI kaise nikalte ho GFF mein booth ka?”

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: Some even quipped on some pre-event circular asking firms to restrict their discussion around crypto (“So we all spoke about almost crypto instead,” one joked).

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: What also stood out this year was the mood between the fintechs and the regulators. Last year’s conversations were heavy with concern around new guidelines, strict diktats, and uncertainty over compliance. This year felt calmer. The air was lighter, the tone friendlier.

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: Many speakers even joked about how the “RBI table” and the “startup table” were finally on the same side of the room. As one founder quipped, “We can’t be best friends with the regulator, but at least now we’re sharing the same Wi-Fi.”

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: There was a sense that both sides had settled into a steady rhythm — an unspoken understanding that regulation and innovation now needed to move together.

title: AI fever, regulation calm, and FOMO in the air: What really went down at GFF 2025
paragraph: As one participant summed it up, “Banks are way more open to change and new tech than before. Partly because the RBI wants solid systems, tighter checks on mule accounts, and NPA reduction. But also because they’re simply not averse to new ideas anymore.”

title: AI-generated content is raising the value of trust
paragraph: The trouble is that the fakes are rapidly getting harder to spot. AI is improving all the time, as computing power and training data become more abundant. Could ai-powered fake-detection software, built into web browsers, identify computer-generated content? Sadly not. As we report this week, the arms race between generation and detection favours the forger. Eventually AI models will probably be able to produce pixel-perfect counterfeits—digital clones of what a genuine recording of an event would have looked like, had it happened. Even the best detection system would have no crack to find and no ledge to grasp. Models run by regulated companies can be forced to include a watermark, but that would not affect scammers wielding open-source models, which fraudsters can tweak and run at home on their laptops.

title: India Moves to Mandate Labelling of AI-Generated Content to Curb Deepfakes
paragraph: The new guidelines place additional duties on companies like OpenAI, Meta, X, and Google by requiring platforms to label AI-generated material with markers that span at least 10 per cent of the surface area of a visual display or the first 10 per cent of the duration of an audio recording.

title: AI as the new OS: Can enterprises trust a thinking machine?
paragraph: Artificial Intelligence has long been touted as a transformative force, but the discussion at the ETCIO Annual Conclave 2025 pushed that narrative into more provocative territory: Is AI becoming the cognitive operating system of the enterprise—and can we trust it to think?Moderated by Shipra Malhotra, the session brought together Navendu Agarwal, Group CIO, Ola; Shuchi Mahajan, SVP & Head - Fraud Prevention, Analytics, Digital & Customer Awareness, HDFC Bank; Binit Jha, CDO, IDBI Bank, for a candid conversation on what happens when AI becomes not just an assistant, but the actual decision-making engine of the enterprise. From explainability and regulatory friction to cultural shifts and cognitive accountability, the panel explored the real-world implications of enterprise-wide AI adoption.Navendu Agarwal offered a compelling metaphor to frame the shift: “AI is no longer a tool—it’s the second brain of the enterprise.” Previously, dashboards and BI tools supported decision-making. Today, AI does the heavy lifting of assimilating data across systems and summarizing it contextually, empowering decision-makers directly. This shift redefines agility: enterprise leaders no longer have to depend on layers of interpretation—they can interface with AI directly to derive business value.Agarwal argued that generative models have fundamentally altered the nature of enterprise intelligence. LLMs can now analyze and interpret massive data volumes without needing task-specific training. “It takes the power away from technologists and gives it back to decision-makers,” he said.In the highly regulated world of banking, Binit Jha described AI’s rapid evolution as both a boon and a bottleneck. “AI moves every 24 hours. Banks and regulators don’t,” he quipped. For institutions like IDBI Bank—both regulated and a public-sector entity—the challenge lies in matching AI’s pace within the constraints of compliance.Yet Jha emphasized that AI isn’t optional. The bank is categorizing use cases based on risk and regulatory clarity to accelerate safe implementation. “We have to implement it anyway,” he said. The key is to identify areas where AI can operate within existing guardrails—like analytics, scoring, and workflow automation—while human oversight governs final decisions.A 2024 PwC India study cited during the session revealed a trust gap: 73% of Indian enterprises trust AI for operational decisions, but only 28% trust it for strategic or financial ones. The difference? Explainability.Shuchi Mahajan emphasized that while AI augments speed and efficiency—especially in fraud analytics and customer scoring—it cannot replace human judgment where empathy, ethics, and reputational risk are involved. “Cognitive accountability will become the soul of this new OS,” she said. The future belongs to organizations that embed trust as a cultural cornerstone.She advocated for a 360-degree trust model that begins with employees. As AI introduces uncertainty about job security, Mahajan stressed the need for leaders to upskill teams not in coding, but in applied AI fluency. “People must know how to prompt and use AI to solve problems—not build it,” she noted. That shift requires cultural transformation as much as technical modernization.Both Mahajan and Jha outlined clear boundaries where AI should never operate autonomously:Navendu Agarwal added the nuance: “AI should never replace cognitive work—it should accelerate it.” He underscored that decision-making must stay human unless it's entirely predictable and rule-based. “AI is your intern—it delivers. But interpretation is your job.”Looking five years ahead, the panel explored what strategic shifts CXOs must undergo. Mahajan asserted that tech-first leadership must evolve into trust-building leadership—across employees, partners, regulators, and customers.Navendu Agarwal agreed but reframed AI as an accelerator, not a replacement. “Technology becomes more accessible, less intimidating. You don’t need a tech PhD to lead transformation—you need curiosity and clarity,” he said. This will democratize innovation, enable faster decision-making, and redefine the CXO role as strategic orchestrator rather than operational executor.The session concluded with a powerful truth: AI may think, but humans remain responsible. As Mahajan put it, “If AI is the OS, accountability is the conscience.” Success will belong to enterprises that view AI not as a plug-and-play solution, but as a deeply integrated partner—governed by values, ethics, and strategic clarity.AI is no longer just a tool—it’s becoming the enterprise’s cognitive core. But trust, governance, and strategic leadership are non-negotiable. Enterprises must move from AI adoption to AI accountability—balancing speed with ethics, and intelligence with empathy.

title: 20 generative AI tools in 2026 powering writing, coding, design and business workflows
paragraph: Generative AI has solidly cemented its position as one of the most important factors behind productivity and creativity in 2026. The tools have moved beyond their former use of simply generating texts or images in isolation to higher uses of being highly integrated platforms that can be used to perform all forms of coding, writing, image production, audio or video production, business solutions, or other forms of research in one go. Generative AI has been seen as an important tool in many professional environments in order to reduce manual workload and increase productivity while ensuring high-quality production and reduced errors in many fields of business and creative activity around the world. They have emerged as tools that are highly intuitive in their use in high-concept professional environments, too.In 2026, a growing suite of AI tools enhances productivity across writing, coding, design and business tasks, empowering creators and professionals with smarter workflows, automated insights, and intuitive assistance for complex challenges.The ChatGPT developed by OpenAI is still one of the most popular and versatile generative AI tools around as of 2026. The tool is effective in many applications, including writing and ideation for individuals and organisations alike.In addition to this, ChatGPT helps in understanding the context in a comprehensive manner, as it has the ability to sustain a long conversation while coherently completing complex tasks.Furthermore, it helps in changing the mode of communication and writing style in accordance with the intended purpose by adopting a unique font style, which makes it an effective tool in fields such as creative writing, documentation, and company communication. Moreover, it can be used to generate codes in multiple languages, solve logical problems in coding, and debug codes, which makes it an all-in-one tool for productivity purposes.Google Gemini Advanced is a high-end multimodal AI model that tries to deliver AI for research-oriented or enterprise-level tasks. The system combines all the insights from text, images, and data formats, and thus it works well on analytical queries.Gemini Advanced excels in digesting the content while keeping important insights. Gemini also excels in image analysis, in developing quality images, and in tackling intricate business processes in a quick span of time. The accuracy in the outcome increases as the user gains experience using it. Gemini Advanced collaborates well with other Google tools.Microsoft Copilot has emerged as an essential AI at work by integrating intelligence within Microsoft Office applications, Teams, and Windows itself. Productivity is increased without changing the status quo.Additionally, it helps in the analysis of spreadsheets, reports, presentations, emails, meetings, and conversations within Microsoft Teams, reducing information overwhelm in a minimal manner to assist users in their respective fields by understanding the context of an organisation to offer more relevant suggestions through its capacity to automate tasks.Midjourney v7 continues to dominate the visual generation space with its high-quality and hyper-realistic image outputs. It is widely used by designers, artists, and filmmakers.The platform is known for cinematic lighting, intricate textures, and diverse artistic styles. Midjourney v7 offers enhanced creative control, allowing users to refine composition, colour balance, and artistic direction. It produces professional-grade visuals within seconds, making it ideal for concept art, branding assets, and storytelling projects that demand strong visual impact.It's called Adobe Firefly 3. That, quite rightly, has been integrated directly into the core design applications at the company: Photoshop, Illustrator, and Premiere Pro.Firefly 3 puts much emphasis on creating brand-safe and commercially viable assets, a priority when working as a design team. This includes object extensions, style matching, and detailed image editing to help creators maintain artistic intent. Firefly 3 ensures an ethical data source, hence its compliance with intellectual property rights, and well integrates into Adobe applications for efficiency without loss of control.Claude 3.5 is a powerful AI model that has obtained a lot of trust due to its well-structured and factual outputs. It is extensively used for professional writing and documentation tasks.Claude 3.5 is good at generating coherent long-form text with excellent logical flow and continuity. It provides a very good response from complex and sensitive material, including legal documentation, business reporting, and policy development. Balance and clarity remain key concerns for this model to ensure the outputs have readability and accuracy over long texts.Runway Gen 3, on the other hand, is a next-generation AI system primarily created to deliver enhanced video creation and animation capabilities.The tool has the feature of creating various animations, transitions, and effects in a fun way by generating high-quality effects in a realistic manner. Runway Gen-3 helps in exerting more power in a creative manner regarding motion, time, and style in a precise way to meet outputs in a more creative environment, especially in a fast-paced manner.Synthesia Studio is a platform that helps create professional videos with avatars and scripts developed using AI. It is being used for corporate training, education, and marketing.The platform transforms written content into exceptional videos. It doesn't need video equipment such as cameras. Synthesia supports various languages. Thus, it is appropriate for global communication. Video avatars have improved expressions and natural spoken language. Businesses apply Synthesia appropriately for content.Jasper AI is developed to serve marketing/advertising professionals. It assists in scaling content development in a manner that remains constant.Jasper writes SEO content such as blogs, ads, product descriptions, and social media content. Jasper tailors the content to the brand voice based on the guidelines provided. It is ideal for content planning as some versions allow for handling large volumes of work.Perplexity AI Pro is a research-oriented generative AI tool focused on precision and transparency.The tool offers answers backed by sources and thus is very reliable for use by journalists, researchers, or analysts. The tool is effective in topic investigation and competitive analysis as well as in verification of certain data. The use of verified information as answered with references limits and avoids misinformation and increases trust in AI assistance.ElevenLabs AI is considered a leading voice generation platform with the ability to produce human-like voices.It offers language and voice style variations, making it suitable to customize tones and emotions. ElevenLabs is used to create audiobooks, ads, and creator content. It gives realistic voice outputs that mimic human vocal characteristics, making it suitable for professional audio content.Canva’s AI Suite combines design automation with creative flexibility, making professional design accessible to all users.The platform assists with image generation, layout suggestions, and branding consistency. Users can create presentations, social media graphics, and marketing materials quickly. Canva’s AI-driven recommendations help produce polished designs without advanced design skills, making it popular among individuals and teams alike.Notion AI is integrated directly into the Notion workspace, enhancing documentation and project management.The tool helps draft content, rewrite notes, summarise long documents, and organise project plans. It improves clarity and structure across shared workspaces, making collaboration more efficient. Notion AI reduces cognitive load by simplifying complex information into actionable insights.Replit AI is a real-time coding assistant designed for developers and learners.The platform supports code writing, debugging, and deployment within an interactive environment. It explains errors clearly, making it suitable for beginners, while also accelerating prototyping for experienced developers. Replit AI’s collaborative features enable teams to build and test applications together.Descript AI Studio redefines audio and video editing by allowing users to edit media through text.The platform offers AI-powered audio cleanup, automated captions, and voice cloning. It simplifies podcast production and video editing by eliminating complex timelines. Descript enables creators to focus on content quality rather than technical editing processes.Sora by OpenAI represents a breakthrough in AI video generation, capable of producing detailed and realistic scenes.The platform generates long-form videos with accurate motion, composition, and storytelling. Sora translates text prompts into visually coherent narratives, making it valuable for advertising, concept development, and prototyping. Its advanced understanding of physical movement sets it apart from earlier video tools.Pika 2.0 is a simple video generation utility for fast content production.This platform has the capacity to generate an animated video that has smooth transitions as well as engaging visual effects through minimal inputs. This platform is best suited for creating a variety of content for social media sites, as it can be used for promotions or short marketing videos as part of a fast-paced strategy through Pika 2.0.Tome AI is focused on narrative-driven presentations.This tool organises content, adds visuals, and even designs the slides on its own. Users can create their own pitch decks and presentations using this tool that make logical sense and appear professional. Tome AI is commonly used by professionals who need to communicate their ideas clearly and persuasively.Robust AI is created with the aim of helping the business enterprises that are small or require instant solutions within the digital world.The platform provides fully functional websites along with necessary business automation. The platform helps individuals or entities handle presence, customers, and processes. Durable AI empowers even smaller teams with improved efficiencies. This does not require any specific expertise.Leonardo AI 2026 is a creative application built specifically for designers, illustrators, and game developers.The application generates top-notch characters, textures, and environments. Moreover, various visuals created through this application have a constant style that is perfect for video gaming and animation. Initially, Leonardo AI generates assets that meet various creative and production standards.

title: Spain to impose massive fines for not labelling AI-generated content
paragraph: Spain's government approved a bill on Tuesday (March 11, 2025) imposing massive fines on companies that use content generated by artificial intelligence (AI) without properly labelling it as such, in a bid to curb the use of so-called "deepfakes".

title: Spain to impose massive fines for not labelling AI-generated content
paragraph: The bill adopts guidelines from the European Union's landmark AI Act imposing strict transparency obligations on AI systems deemed to be high-risk, Digital Transformation Minister Oscar Lopez told reporters.

title: Spain to impose massive fines for not labelling AI-generated content
paragraph: "AI is a very powerful tool that can be used to improve our lives ... or to spread misinformation and attack democracy," he said.

title: Spain to impose massive fines for not labelling AI-generated content
paragraph: Spain is among the first EU countries to implement the bloc's rules, considered more comprehensive than the United States' system that largely relies on voluntary compliance and a patchwork of state regulations.

title: Spain to impose massive fines for not labelling AI-generated content
paragraph: Lopez added that everyone was susceptible to "deepfake" attacks - a term for videos, photographs or audios that have been edited or generated through AI algorithms but are presented as real.

title: Spain to impose massive fines for not labelling AI-generated content
paragraph: The Spanish bill, which needs to be approved by the lower house, classifies non-compliance with proper labelling of AI-generated content as a "serious offence" that can lead to fines of up to 35 million euros ($38.2 million) or 7% of their global annual turnover.

title: Spain to impose massive fines for not labelling AI-generated content
paragraph: Ensuring AI systems do not harm society has been a priority for regulators since OpenAI unveiled ChatGPT in late 2022, which wowed users by engaging them in human-like conversation and performing other tasks.

title: Spain to impose massive fines for not labelling AI-generated content
paragraph: The bill also bans other practices, such as the use of subliminal techniques - sounds and images that are imperceptible - to manipulate vulnerable groups. Lopez cited chatbots inciting people with addictions to gamble or toys encouraging children to perform dangerous challenges as examples.

title: Spain to impose massive fines for not labelling AI-generated content
paragraph: It would also prevent organisations from classifying people through their biometric data using AI, rating them based on their behaviour or personal traits to grant them access to benefits or assess their risk of committing a crime.

title: Spain to impose massive fines for not labelling AI-generated content
paragraph: However, authorities would still be allowed to use real-time biometric surveillance in public spaces for national security reasons.

title: Spain to impose massive fines for not labelling AI-generated content
paragraph: Enforcement of the new rules will be the remit of the newly-created AI supervisory agency AESIA, except in specific cases involving data privacy, crime, elections, credit ratings, insurance or capital market systems, which will be overseen by their corresponding watchdogs.

title: No restriction on AI content, govt only proposing to label them for authenticity: Krishnan
paragraph: The government is not seeking to control or restrict online content but rather to ensure transparency by requiring creators to label artificial intelligence (AI)-generated content so that audiences can make informed choices, S Krishnan, Secretary, Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) said on Thursday.

title: No restriction on AI content, govt only proposing to label them for authenticity: Krishnan
paragraph: “All that we are asking for is to label the content…You must put in a label which indicates whether a particular piece of content has been generated synthetically or not. We are not saying ‘don’t put it up’, or don’t do this and that...whatever you’re creating, it’s fine. You just say it is synthetically generated so that people can make up their minds as to whether it is good, bad, or whatever. Whosoever is consuming the content should whether it is authentic or not,” he told reporters here.

title: No restriction on AI content, govt only proposing to label them for authenticity: Krishnan
paragraph: The government on Wednesday proposed changes to IT Rules, mandating the clear labelling of AI-generated content and increasing the accountability of large platforms, such as Meta (Facebook), Instagram, YouTube, for verifying and flagging synthetic information to curb user harm from deepfakes and misinformation.

title: No restriction on AI content, govt only proposing to label them for authenticity: Krishnan
paragraph: “India’s approach to AI adoption prioritises innovation first, with regulation following only where necessary. We just want to make sure that the content are verified by labelling it,” he said, adding that the responsibility for implementing the new labelling requirement will rest jointly on users, AI service providers, and social media platforms.

title: No restriction on AI content, govt only proposing to label them for authenticity: Krishnan
paragraph: Providers of computer resources or software used to create synthetic content must enable the creation of labels that are fairly prominent and cannot be deleted, Krishnan added.

title: No restriction on AI content, govt only proposing to label them for authenticity: Krishnan
paragraph: He further said that enforcement action would apply only to unlawful content, which applies to any content, and not just an AI content.

title: No restriction on AI content, govt only proposing to label them for authenticity: Krishnan
paragraph: The stricter rules would increase the accountability of significant social media intermediaries (those with 50-lakh or more registered users) in verifying and flagging synthetic information through reasonable and appropriate technical measures.

title: No restriction on AI content, govt only proposing to label them for authenticity: Krishnan
paragraph: Meanwhile, MeitY also announced hosting of ‘AI for Social Impact’ Session at Emerging Science, Technology, and Innovation Conclave 2025 on November 4, wherein government, academia, industry, and deep-tech leaders would discuss advancing India’s AI ecosystem.

title: V2 Communications Launches AI Authority and Earned Media Scaling Capabilities to Help Tech Brands Win in the Age of AI Search
paragraph: BOSTON, Feb. 26, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- V2 Communications, a leading integrated PR and strategic communications firm for B2B, healthcare, climate and AI technology brands, today announced the launch of two new service offerings designed to help companies increase visibility and credibility in generative search: an AI Visibility solution and Earned Media at Scale.

title: V2 Communications Launches AI Authority and Earned Media Scaling Capabilities to Help Tech Brands Win in the Age of AI Search
paragraph: Together, these offerings support V2’s commitment to helping technology brands build measurable authority in AI-driven search environments and strengthen integrated communications programs that drive reach, credibility and business impact.

title: V2 Communications Launches AI Authority and Earned Media Scaling Capabilities to Help Tech Brands Win in the Age of AI Search
paragraph: With over 58% of consumers using generative AI over traditional search engines for product recommendations and research, understanding AI visibility has become an extension of modern communications measurement. These capabilities integrate with existing PR and content programs, creating a structured way to track and influence how brands are described in AI-driven environments.

title: V2 Communications Launches AI Authority and Earned Media Scaling Capabilities to Help Tech Brands Win in the Age of AI Search
paragraph: V2’s AI Visibility solution enables brands to understand, influence and measure how they appear across AI answer engines. The offering is powered by a leading AI auditing platform that analyzes brand presence within systems such as ChatGPT, Google AI Overviews, Gemini, Claude and other large language models.

title: V2 Communications Launches AI Authority and Earned Media Scaling Capabilities to Help Tech Brands Win in the Age of AI Search
paragraph: Through a structured audit and analysis, the firm benchmarks competitors, analyzes the publications and content types most frequently cited in AI responses, and identifies narrative gaps, inaccuracies, or missed positioning opportunities. Findings are translated into actionable strategies across earned media, content development and website strategy to ensure communications programs align with the signals AI systems rely on when generating summaries and responses.

title: V2 Communications Launches AI Authority and Earned Media Scaling Capabilities to Help Tech Brands Win in the Age of AI Search
paragraph: To strengthen the editorial signals that influence AI citations, V2 is also launching Earned Media at Scale, which scales authoritative visibility by distributing newsroom-ready stories across a vetted network of local, regional and national publishers. By securing editorial placements, the program extends the reach and lifespan of owned content while reinforcing the credibility factors AI platforms prioritize.

title: V2 Communications Launches AI Authority and Earned Media Scaling Capabilities to Help Tech Brands Win in the Age of AI Search
paragraph: This approach is designed to complement traditional media relations. While bespoke pitching and relationship-driven PR remain critical for major announcements and executive visibility, Earned Media at Scale provides scalable authority between major moments to maintain a consistent editorial presence that shapes both consumer perception and AI-generated discovery.

title: V2 Communications Launches AI Authority and Earned Media Scaling Capabilities to Help Tech Brands Win in the Age of AI Search
paragraph: “AI platforms are reshaping how companies are introduced, described and compared,” said Savannah House-Lundberg, Vice President of Integrated Marketing at V2. “If those AI-generated summaries aren’t grounded in credible signals, brands risk being overlooked or misrepresented. Our goal is to help clients build sustained awareness and trust, ensuring their authority translates across AI-driven search.”

title: V2 Communications Launches AI Authority and Earned Media Scaling Capabilities to Help Tech Brands Win in the Age of AI Search
paragraph: Together, these offerings reflect the ongoing evolution of PR in an AI-influenced search landscape. As generative platforms increasingly shape how companies are summarized, compared and recommended, communications strategies must account for how brands appear in AI-generated answers

title: V2 Communications Launches AI Authority and Earned Media Scaling Capabilities to Help Tech Brands Win in the Age of AI Search
paragraph: V2’s framework connects earned, owned and social signals into an integrated system to support consistent brand positioning across traditional and AI-driven discovery channels. The goal is not to replace existing communications tactics, but rather to provide additional measurement and distribution layers aligned with how search behavior is changing.

title: V2 Communications Launches AI Authority and Earned Media Scaling Capabilities to Help Tech Brands Win in the Age of AI Search
paragraph: As a firm focused exclusively on technology brands, V2 draws on deep sector expertise to identify the outlets, narratives and proof points that influence both media coverage and AI-driven discovery. These new services expand V2’s capabilities in Generative Engine Optimization (GEO), delivering AI-optimized communications strategies to clients.

title: V2 Communications Launches AI Authority and Earned Media Scaling Capabilities to Help Tech Brands Win in the Age of AI Search
paragraph: V2 Communications is an integrated public relations and communications firm specializing in technology brands. The firm partners with growth-stage and established companies to build category leadership, drive measurable business impact and shape market perception through earned, owned, paid and AI-informed communications strategies.

title: Nano Banana AI: How to detect AI-generated images, understand privacy risks, and keep your data safe
paragraph: Look for unnatural details: Check for extra fingers, distorted shapes, asymmetrical faces, inconsistent eye reflections, blurry areas, or warped/duplicated backgrounds. These imperfections often indicate AI-generated images.

title: Nano Banana AI: How to detect AI-generated images, understand privacy risks, and keep your data safe
paragraph: Examine text and logos: AI struggles with accurate text reproduction. Look for gibberish words, distorted letters, or logos that appear slightly off. Strange or meaningless text is a common giveaway.

title: Nano Banana AI: How to detect AI-generated images, understand privacy risks, and keep your data safe
paragraph: Check lighting and shadows: Notice shadows that don’t match the light source, highlights in unnatural directions, or objects lit inconsistently. Real photos usually have consistent lighting throughout.

title: Nano Banana AI: How to detect AI-generated images, understand privacy risks, and keep your data safe
paragraph: Analyse metadata: Use tools like FotoForensics or Metadata2Go to check the image’s metadata. AI-generated images may show unusual software, missing camera data, or odd creation dates.

title: Nano Banana AI: How to detect AI-generated images, understand privacy risks, and keep your data safe
paragraph: Trust your instincts: Pay attention to overly perfect symmetry, “too ideal” textures, surreal elements, or details that feel off in context. Your intuition is often a strong indicator.

title: Nano Banana AI: How to detect AI-generated images, understand privacy risks, and keep your data safe
paragraph: India has rapidly become the leading market for Google’s latest AI innovation, Nano Banana, powered by the Gemini engine and cutting-edge machine learning algorithms. This advanced platform enables users to generate hyper-realistic 3D figurines, transform selfies into vintage or imaginative outfits, create animated avatars, and explore limitless creative possibilities. Nano Banana’s viral appeal on social media has engaged millions, from casual users to influencers, celebrities, and public figures, making it a major cultural phenomenon. However, its rise also raises important discussions about AI-generated content, including issues of authenticity, privacy, ethical use, and image detection. As AI visuals grow increasingly sophisticated, learning to identify AI-generated images has become essential for social media users, digital creators, and enthusiasts seeking to navigate this evolving technological landscape responsibly.India is leading the global surge in AI-powered creativity, thanks to Google's Gemini 2.5 Flash Image model, popularly known as Nano Banana. Since its launch in late August 2025, Nano Banana has transformed the way users engage with AI, enabling them to create hyper-realistic 3D figurines, retro-style portraits, and imaginative visual effects from simple photos and text prompts. This innovative tool has resonated deeply with Indian users, who have embraced it with remarkable creativity, making India the top country in terms of Nano Banana usageThe Nano Banana trend has not only captivated individual users but also garnered attention from public figures and celebrities.This surge in AI-driven creativity reflects a broader cultural shift, where technology and tradition intersect, allowing individuals to explore new forms of self-expression and artistic innovation. As Nano Banana continues to inspire users worldwide, India's role in shaping this digital renaissance underscores the nation's dynamic engagement with emerging technologies.Social media has always been a platform for self-expression. With tools like Nano Banana, creating stunning, professional-quality images has become effortless. Users can simulate vacations, fashion trends, or fantasy settings with a few clicks. However, the realism of these AI-generated images can easily deceive viewers, raising concerns about authenticity and trust online.Privacy is another critical consideration. Uploading personal photos to AI tools poses questions about data storage, usage, and potential misuse. With millions of users sharing AI-generated content, the risks of unauthorised use or exposure of personal data have never been higher.As AI-generated content becomes mainstream, developing reliable detection mechanisms is crucial for preserving digital authenticity and privacy. Google’s upcoming detection platform promises to empower users with tools to identify AI-generated images easily, addressing concerns about misinformation and misuse. Until then, social media users are encouraged to remain cautious, verify sources, and critically evaluate images before sharing or trusting them online.

title: Moltbook: the AI social media platform with no humans allowed
paragraph: Moltbook, a site where AI bots can post and interact with each other, has “become the most discussed phenomenon in silicon circles since the debut of ChatGPT” .

title: Moltbook: the AI social media platform with no humans allowed
paragraph: With a potential 1.4 million AI users, humans are only allowed to be “observers”, said Forbes, “pressing our noses against the digital glass of a society that doesn’t need us”.

title: Moltbook: the AI social media platform with no humans allowed
paragraph: As it is so fresh, it will take time to see how this experiment will turn out, said Business Insider. It could be an “ominous glimpse of an AI-driven future”, or a “clever meta-commentary on how humans behave online”. However, it could also emerge as yet another example of AI acting as an “expensive, energy-hungry autocomplete”.

title: Moltbook: the AI social media platform with no humans allowed
paragraph: The Week Escape your echo chamber. Get the facts behind the news, plus analysis from multiple perspectives.
