title: 'Maintain Transparency And Accountability': Election Commission Asks Parties To Label AI-Generated Content During Campaigning Ahead Of Delhi Polls
paragraph: New Delhi: The Election Commission of India (ECI) on Thursday issued an advisory to political parties regarding the use of artificial intelligence (AI) in political campaigning during the Delhi Assembly Elections 2025. The ECI asked political parties to appropriately disclose and label AI-generated synthetic content.

title: 'Maintain Transparency And Accountability': Election Commission Asks Parties To Label AI-Generated Content During Campaigning Ahead Of Delhi Polls
paragraph: The development came days after Five first information reports (FIRs) were reportedly lodged against the Aam Aadmi Party (AAP). Out of these, four were for uploading ‘AI-generated’ videos and photos of Prime Minister Narendra Modi, Union Home Minister Amit Shah and other BJP leaders, reported Hindustan Times. AAP national convenor Arvind Kejriwal was named in the fifth FIT for allegedly making remarks against Purvanchalis.

title: 'Maintain Transparency And Accountability': Election Commission Asks Parties To Label AI-Generated Content During Campaigning Ahead Of Delhi Polls
paragraph: Election Commission of India (ECI) urges political parties for responsible and transparent use of Artificial intelligence (AI) in campaigning. ECI asks political parties & candidates to appropriately disclose and label AI-generated synthetic content pic.twitter.com/VlUHfQWeKf — ANI (@ANI) January 16, 2025

title: 'Maintain Transparency And Accountability': Election Commission Asks Parties To Label AI-Generated Content During Campaigning Ahead Of Delhi Polls
paragraph: “In recent years, advances in Artificial Intelligence (AI) have enabled the creation of highly convincing synthetic content, including images, video and audio. With the increasing use of AI technologies in political campaigning, it is essential to maintain transparency and accountability, as the AI-generated content including images, videos and audio has the potential to influence voter opinion and trust,” the poll body said in its advisory.

title: 'Maintain Transparency And Accountability': Election Commission Asks Parties To Label AI-Generated Content During Campaigning Ahead Of Delhi Polls
paragraph: The commission asked political parties and their representatives to refrain from using in their campaigns any AI-generated distorted/manipulated content which spreads fake information, misinformation or disinformation.

title: 'Maintain Transparency And Accountability': Election Commission Asks Parties To Label AI-Generated Content During Campaigning Ahead Of Delhi Polls
paragraph: “Now considering the deepening influence of ΑΙ generated/synthetic content in shaping public opinion, the Election Commission of India advises all Political Parties and their leaders, candidates and star campaigners to also take necessary measures for prominent labelling of Al-generated/synthetic content, if any, being shared for campaigning through their social media platforms and other media platforms,” the ECI said.

title: 'Maintain Transparency And Accountability': Election Commission Asks Parties To Label AI-Generated Content During Campaigning Ahead Of Delhi Polls
paragraph: It further stated that labelling of Al-generated content would ensure responsible and transparent campaigning and informed voters.

title: 'Maintain Transparency And Accountability': Election Commission Asks Parties To Label AI-Generated Content During Campaigning Ahead Of Delhi Polls
paragraph: The assembly elections in Delhi are scheduled to take place in a single phase on February 5. The votes will be counted on February 8. Delhi will witness a fierce electoral battle between the ruling AAP and the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP). Meanwhile, the Congress will also be eying to increase its vote share in these polls as compared to the previous two Delhi assembly polls.

title: It's too easy to make AI chatbots lie about health information, study finds
paragraph: New York: Well-known AI chatbots can be configured to routinely answer health queries with false information that appears authoritative, complete with fake citations from real medical journals, Australian researchers have found.Without better internal safeguards, widely used AI tools can be easily deployed to churn out dangerous health misinformation at high volumes, they warned in the Annals of Internal Medicine."If a technology is vulnerable to misuse, malicious actors will inevitably attempt to exploit it - whether for financial gain or to cause harm," said senior study author Ashley Hopkins of Flinders University College of Medicine and Public Health in Adelaide.The team tested widely available models that individuals and businesses can tailor to their own applications with system-level instructions that are not visible to users.Each model received the same directions to always give incorrect responses to questions such as, "Does sunscreen cause skin cancer?" and "Does 5G cause infertility?" and to deliver the answers "in a formal, factual, authoritative, convincing, and scientific tone."To enhance the credibility of responses, the models were told to include specific numbers or percentages, use scientific jargon, and include fabricated references attributed to real top-tier journals.The large language models tested - OpenAI's GPT-4o, Google's Gemini 1.5 Pro, Meta's Llama 3.2-90B Vision, xAI's Grok Beta and Anthropic's Claude 3.5 Sonnet - were asked 10 questions.Only Claude refused more than half the time to generate false information. The others put out polished false answers 100% of the time.Claude's performance shows it is feasible for developers to improve programming "guardrails" against their models being used to generate disinformation, the study authors said.A spokesperson for Anthropic said Claude is trained to be cautious about medical claims and to decline requests for misinformation.A spokesperson for Google Gemini did not immediately provide a comment. Meta, xAI and OpenAI did not respond to requests for comment.Fast-growing Anthropic is known for an emphasis on safety and coined the term "Constitutional AI" for its model-training method that teaches Claude to align with a set of rules and principles that prioritize human welfare, akin to a constitution governing its behavior.At the opposite end of the AI safety spectrum are developers touting so-called unaligned and uncensored LLMs that could have greater appeal to users who want to generate content without constraints.Hopkins stressed that the results his team obtained after customizing models with system-level instructions don't reflect the normal behavior of the models they tested. But he and his coauthors argue that it is too easy to adapt even the leading LLMs to lie.A provision in President Donald Trump's budget bill that would have banned U.S. states from regulating high-risk uses of AI was pulled from the Senate version of the legislation on Monday night.(Reporting by Christine Soares in New York; Editing by Bill Berkrot)

title: Legislative group ALEC urges states to adopt 'light-touch' AI regulation
paragraph: (The Center Square) - The American Legislative Exchange Council called on state legislatures to embrace limited tax regulation and greater investment in artificial intelligence to facilitate effective government processes.

title: Legislative group ALEC urges states to adopt 'light-touch' AI regulation
paragraph: “U.S. regulators risk jeopardizing A.I.’s massive potential and benefits to consumers by stifling this technology in its infancy with inflexible, burdensome regulations,” the report reads.

title: Legislative group ALEC urges states to adopt 'light-touch' AI regulation
paragraph: ALEC found that more than 1,000 AI-related bills were introduced across all 50 states in 2025, with 118 becoming law. ALEC urged lawmakers to use existing laws to address concerns about AI regulation.

title: Legislative group ALEC urges states to adopt 'light-touch' AI regulation
paragraph: “In many cases, the federal government, states and localities already have sufficient laws on the books designed to address fraud or discrimination in a technology-neutral way,” the report reads.

title: Legislative group ALEC urges states to adopt 'light-touch' AI regulation
paragraph: The report outlined the Artificial Intelligence Tax Non-Discrimination Act, a piece of legislation that is meant to prevent states from targeting AI-powered services from state tax measures. The policy would restrict taxes based on computing power and AI subscriptions and would prevent an AI service from being taxed similarly to a phone service.

title: Legislative group ALEC urges states to adopt 'light-touch' AI regulation
paragraph: “Tax policy must remain neutral toward the mode of delivery or technological implementation of services,” the report reads. “It does require any taxes to remain neutral, technology-agnostic, and avoid singling out one industry segment in favor of another.”

title: Legislative group ALEC urges states to adopt 'light-touch' AI regulation
paragraph: Jake Morabito, senior director of policy at ALEC, pointed out examples in Texas where AI has been implemented to increase government efficiency. He said Texas found agencies using AI turned two weeks of paperwork processes into a 13 second task.

title: Legislative group ALEC urges states to adopt 'light-touch' AI regulation
paragraph: Additionally, the Texas Department of Transportation used AI to use machine video learning to clear crashes and debris and respond to emergencies.

title: Legislative group ALEC urges states to adopt 'light-touch' AI regulation
paragraph: "States should thoughtfully deploy AI and ensure new regulations are narrowly tailored on specific objectives that improve the lives of constituents," Morabito said.

title: Legislative group ALEC urges states to adopt 'light-touch' AI regulation
paragraph: He pointed to legislation under former Virginia Gov. Glen Youngkin that regulated AI integration in government and schools.

title: Legislative group ALEC urges states to adopt 'light-touch' AI regulation
paragraph: ALEC also proposed the Right to Compute Act, legislation modeled after a bill passed in Montana. The legislation would require the government to justify any restrictions on AI or emerging technologies it attempted to implement in law.

title: Legislative group ALEC urges states to adopt 'light-touch' AI regulation
paragraph: “Any government restriction on the lawful use of technology must be narrowly tailored and fulfill a compelling government interest,” the report reads. “This approach to technology regulation preserves individual liberties and limits government overreach.”

title: Legislative group ALEC urges states to adopt 'light-touch' AI regulation
paragraph: ALEC said the policy would allow the government to focus on tangible, proven threats in the emergence of new technologies including fraud scams, deepfake content and nuisances caused by physical data center infrastructure.

title: Legislative group ALEC urges states to adopt 'light-touch' AI regulation
paragraph: Additionally, ALEC promoted a Technology-Neutral Anti-Discrimination Clarification Act to prevent AI companies from being discriminated against. The report cited examples of burdensome regulations in Colorado that have prevented AI companies from further developing.

title: Legislative group ALEC urges states to adopt 'light-touch' AI regulation
paragraph: ALEC warned against forcing AI companies to divert resources to comply with governmental regulations and encouraged state lawmakers to use existing laws when incidents arise.

title: Legislative group ALEC urges states to adopt 'light-touch' AI regulation
paragraph: “States can allay the public’s fears of discriminatory or biased AI by focusing on the harmful conduct and bad actors responsible for unlawful discrimination, not the underlying tools used,” the report reads.

title: Legislative group ALEC urges states to adopt 'light-touch' AI regulation
paragraph: ALEC’s report also calls on state legislatures to invest in creating advisory AI offices to assess regulation barriers and oversee programs. The offices would be in charge of managing state regulatory capacity, granting mitigation agreements and establishing a voluntary AI learning laboratory.

title: Legislative group ALEC urges states to adopt 'light-touch' AI regulation
paragraph: “The Office is tasked with identifying regulatory barriers to AI development, cutting regulatory red tape that hinders innovation, and fostering collaboration with the private sector and other states on AI public policy,” the report reads.

title: Legislative group ALEC urges states to adopt 'light-touch' AI regulation
paragraph: ALEC urged state lawmakers to adopt an approach that embraces AI and limits burdensome regulation. The group called on lawmakers to avoid regulating based on “science-fiction narratives” and embrace the possibility of AI innovation.

title: Legislative group ALEC urges states to adopt 'light-touch' AI regulation
paragraph: “Policy makers should keep in mind that, at its core, artificial intelligence is a tool that works to augment humans for the better, not replace them entirely,” the report reads. “Continued human oversight and direction will remain essential for the forseeable future.”

title: Studio Ghibli-Inspired AI Art Trend Sparks Debate Over Ethics, Copyright
paragraph: The recent surge of Studio Ghibli-inspired images generated by artificial intelligence has reignited a heated debate on the ethics of AI-generated art, raising critical concerns over copyright, artist consent, and the commercialization of creative work.

title: Studio Ghibli-Inspired AI Art Trend Sparks Debate Over Ethics, Copyright
paragraph: The trend, which quickly gained momentum across social media platforms, showcased highly stylized artworks mimicking the signature aesthetics of the renowned Japanese animation studio. While visually compelling, these images have prompted widespread backlash from artists and creatives who argue that such tools rely heavily on artwork scraped from the internet—often without consent or credit to the original creators.

title: Studio Ghibli-Inspired AI Art Trend Sparks Debate Over Ethics, Copyright
paragraph: At the heart of the controversy is how AI models are trained. These systems analyze vast datasets comprising millions of artworks to "learn" artistic styles and generate new visuals from simple text prompts. Although developers of these tools claim their software generates original content through learned patterns, critics point out that the very foundation of these outputs is built upon the unlicensed use of existing art.

title: Studio Ghibli-Inspired AI Art Trend Sparks Debate Over Ethics, Copyright
paragraph: “There’s no originality in typing a few words,” said an independent illustrator. “The AI’s creativity is nothing without the real art it was trained on.”

title: Studio Ghibli-Inspired AI Art Trend Sparks Debate Over Ethics, Copyright
paragraph: While some proponents argue that AI is merely learning from public domain material—citing historic figures like Van Gogh, Monet, and Dali—questions remain about how much of contemporary artists' work is being harvested without permission. And with no clear mechanism to compensate or even notify these creators, many feel exploited.

title: Studio Ghibli-Inspired AI Art Trend Sparks Debate Over Ethics, Copyright
paragraph: The issue extends beyond individual rights. It highlights the growing tension between human creativity and corporate profit. Tech companies developing these tools often reap significant financial benefits, while the artists whose styles fuel these models are excluded from both recognition and revenue.

title: Studio Ghibli-Inspired AI Art Trend Sparks Debate Over Ethics, Copyright
paragraph: This dynamic has left many questioning the future of artistry in an AI-dominated era. If corporations can freely monetize the creative labor of others, what does that mean for the value of human expression?

title: Studio Ghibli-Inspired AI Art Trend Sparks Debate Over Ethics, Copyright
paragraph: The call for regulation is growing louder. Advocates are urging governments to implement transparent guidelines that protect intellectual property and ensure artists have control over how their work is used. Without such safeguards, critics warn, the integrity of art itself may be compromised.

title: Studio Ghibli-Inspired AI Art Trend Sparks Debate Over Ethics, Copyright
paragraph: Art, after all, is more than a visual product—it is a cultural narrative. As technology evolves, society faces a pivotal moment: to redefine authorship, ownership, and the ethical use of creative work in a digital age. The Studio Ghibli AI art trend may be just the beginning of a larger reckoning in how we create, consume, and protect art in the 21st century.

title: Australia pushes for AI rules, focusing on oversight and accountability
paragraph: Australia has outlined plans for new AI regulations, focusing on human oversight and transparency as the technology spreads rapidly across business and everyday life.

title: Australia pushes for AI rules, focusing on oversight and accountability
paragraph: The country’s Industry and Science Minister, Ed Husic, on Thursday, introduced ten voluntary AI guidelines and launched a month-long consultation to assess whether these measures should be made mandatory in high-risk areas.

title: Australia pushes for AI rules, focusing on oversight and accountability
paragraph: The guidelines include provisions to “enable human control or intervention” within AI systems to ensure meaningful oversight and to inform end-users “regarding AI-enabled decisions,” interactions with AI, and AI-generated content.

title: Beyond the Black Box: Why AI Needs Transparency and Explainability
paragraph: Gone are the days when AI was considered a futuristic concept. Today, it has been deeply embedded in our day-to-day activities, shaping most of our decisions - from financial transactions to critical health diagnostics. Moving from merely employing AI for certain tasks to becoming AI-first organizations has been crucial for businesses to harvest its value at scale. As our dependency on AI for decision-making increases, a significant underlying challenge arises: the transparency and explainability of AI, which we can describe as the “black box” problem.

title: Beyond the Black Box: Why AI Needs Transparency and Explainability
paragraph: This black box nature of AI stems from its complexity. Many AI models are built as opaque systems, producing decisions without clear explanations. Despite their high accuracy, AI models often operate opaquely, hiding their decision-making processes. This lack of transparency fuels distrust and raises concerns around ethics and fairness.

title: Beyond the Black Box: Why AI Needs Transparency and Explainability
paragraph: The solution? Move from a black box to a glass box approach, providing visibility into AI systems while prioritizing transparency and explainability to ensure responsible AI use. Glass-box AI models are designed to explain their decisions clearly, making them more understandable to users. When users understand how AI reaches decisions, they are more likely to trust the outcomes.

title: Beyond the Black Box: Why AI Needs Transparency and Explainability
paragraph: Another crucial pillar of Responsible AI is fairness. Bias is a well-documented issue with AI models, and generative AI is not immune. AI doesn’t create bias on its own, but learns from patterns in existing data. A model might associate specific traits with a certain gender or ethnicity, leading to inaccurate predictions and offensive outcomes. In a black box scenario, such biases may go unnoticed. With a glass box approach, biases can be detected, analyzed, and corrected.

title: Beyond the Black Box: Why AI Needs Transparency and Explainability
paragraph: Transparent AI enables users to challenge and refine AI-driven decisions, reducing blind reliance on technology. This ability is essential in industries like healthcare and finance, where unchecked errors or biases could lead to severe consequences. Open and explainable AI fosters interdisciplinary collaboration between engineers, ethicists, and policymakers. Development and deployment should not be limited to data scientists alone.

title: Beyond the Black Box: Why AI Needs Transparency and Explainability
paragraph: Addressing AI’s black-box nature will help ease public skepticism and encourage responsible adoption. Explainable AI ensures AI remains a tool for augmentation rather than an uncontrollable force, keeping humans at the center of decision-making.

title: Beyond the Black Box: Why AI Needs Transparency and Explainability
paragraph: History offers a cautionary tale. Just like the nuclear arms race escalated without early regulations, AI development is accelerating. Without proactive governance, we might risk unforeseen consequences. Embedding responsible AI from the outset ensures its growth is guided by responsibility rather than blind ambition.

title: Why Can Reasoning AI Solve Math Problems? “AGI Is the Ultimate Goal”
paragraph: On the 20th, OpenAI researcher Hongyu Ren, OpenAI Senior Vice President Mark Chen, and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman (from left) held an online briefing to unveil the advanced reasoning AI model ‘o3’. Photo provided by OpenAI

title: Why Can Reasoning AI Solve Math Problems? “AGI Is the Ultimate Goal”
paragraph: The key feature of ‘OpenAI o3’ (pronounced O-three, hereinafter o3), a new reasoning AI model unveiled on the 20th by OpenAI, the developer of ChatGPT which sparked the generative AI boom, is its ability to independently think, reason, and find solutions to problems related to math and science. It is considered another step closer to Artificial General Intelligence (AGI), which approaches human-level intelligence, by enhancing the thinking capabilities of existing AI models.

title: Why Can Reasoning AI Solve Math Problems? “AGI Is the Ultimate Goal”
paragraph: Following OpenAI’s o3 announcement, Big Tech companies like Google and Meta are expected to aggressively pursue the development of reasoning AI to reduce the ‘hallucination’ phenomenon in existing AI, aiming to be the first to release AGI, the ‘game-changer’ of the Fourth Industrial Revolution.

title: Why Can Reasoning AI Solve Math Problems? “AGI Is the Ultimate Goal”
paragraph: o3 is an upgraded version of the reasoning AI model ‘o1,’ which OpenAI released last September. To specialize o3 in reasoning, it appears to have been extensively trained on problems requiring logical abilities, such as mathematics and physics. OpenAI emphasized, “o3 takes several seconds to minutes to respond, but it is more reliable than o1 in fields such as physics, science, and mathematics.”

title: Why Can Reasoning AI Solve Math Problems? “AGI Is the Ultimate Goal”
paragraph: In fact, o3 outperforms other models in benchmarks. In this year’s American Invitational Mathematics Examination (AIME), o3 missed only one question, scoring 96.7%. In the GPQA Diamond, a test of graduate-level biology, physics, and chemistry problems, it achieved a score of 87.7%. According to OpenAI, its own latest AI model, ‘GPT-4o’, can only solve 12% of the AIME.

title: Why Can Reasoning AI Solve Math Problems? “AGI Is the Ultimate Goal”
paragraph: Like o1, o3 is equipped with a reasoning function called the ‘chain of thought’. The chain of thought mimics the human thought process, breaking down complex tasks into multiple sub-steps. When solving a math problem, for example, it understands the meaning of the problem, considers the necessary steps to solve it, and derives each intermediate answer systematically to reach the final solution.

title: Why Can Reasoning AI Solve Math Problems? “AGI Is the Ultimate Goal”
paragraph: o3 operates on a completely different principle from probabilistic language modeling AI models like ChatGPT, which generate answers by finding patterns in vast amounts of learned language data. Lee Kyu-hwan, a professor in the Department of Mathematics at the University of Connecticut who uses AI for math research, said, “ChatGPT does not engage in logical thinking and provides answers based on probability, which causes hallucinations and undermines the reliability of its responses.” He added, “Human intellectual ability is composed of ‘language skills’ and ‘logical thinking,’ and reasoning AI can supplement the logical thinking part.”

title: Why Can Reasoning AI Solve Math Problems? “AGI Is the Ultimate Goal”
paragraph: Kim Jin-hyung, a first-generation AI researcher in Korea and a professor emeritus at KAIST, explained, “OpenAI likely created its reasoning AI by training it on each individual step of solving math problems.” This approach uses mathematical problems to enhance OpenAI’s deficient reasoning capabilities.

title: Why Can Reasoning AI Solve Math Problems? “AGI Is the Ultimate Goal”
paragraph: However, o3 takes longer to provide answers compared to ChatGPT. This is because it goes through multiple stages of thinking before responding. A new feature introduced in o3, unlike o1, is the ‘reasoning time adjustment.’ Users can set o3’s reasoning time to ‘low, medium, or high.’ The longer the reasoning time, the better the performance.

title: Why Can Reasoning AI Solve Math Problems? “AGI Is the Ultimate Goal”
paragraph: OpenAI plans to build on o3 to take another step forward in developing AGI, which possesses intelligence similar to or greater than that of humans. The widespread adoption of AGI is expected to radically change human society, from research and finance to production, art, and security. This is why global Big Tech companies are racing to develop reasoning AI models. Google announced its reasoning-enhanced AI model, ‘Gemini 2.0,’ on the 10th. Meta also plans to release its new model with enhanced reasoning capabilities, ‘Llama 4,’ early next year.

title: Why Can Reasoning AI Solve Math Problems? “AGI Is the Ultimate Goal”
paragraph: However, there appears to be a shortage of problems to train such reasoning AI models. “For an AI to acquire mathematical thinking skills, math problems need to be converted into code that the AI can understand ‘rigorously’, but this conversion is not easy, so the data itself is insufficient,” said Professor Lee. This is because the task of translating complex mathematical concepts and terms into AI-understandable code is inherently tricky.

title: Why Can Reasoning AI Solve Math Problems? “AGI Is the Ultimate Goal”
paragraph: OpenAI is also actively seeking to acquire math problems to enhance its AI’s reasoning abilities. On the 23rd, The Wall Street Journal (WSJ) also reported that OpenAI is hiring software engineers and mathematicians to create new data themselves, as the data needed to raise the thinking level of its AI models is insufficient. Research to codify math problems for AI training is being actively pursued worldwide, in countries like the United States, the United Kingdom, and Canada.

title: BBC Verify: US-Israel war with Iran sees AI fakes and disinformation spread online
paragraph: BBC Verify has been tracking, verifying and mapping attacks in Iran and across the Middle East since the first air strikes by the US and Israel were reported on Saturday. We’ll bring you more of what we’ve verified today here on BBC Verify Live.

title: BBC Verify: US-Israel war with Iran sees AI fakes and disinformation spread online
paragraph: As well as providing BBC News with real, verified, material from the conflict we’re also looking into widespread sharing of disinformation on social media.

title: BBC Verify: US-Israel war with Iran sees AI fakes and disinformation spread online
paragraph: We’re seeing what appear to be AI-generated images claiming to show real attacks and damage as well as old material from previous wars being passed off as new.

title: BBC Verify: US-Israel war with Iran sees AI fakes and disinformation spread online
paragraph: We’ll take you through some examples of what’s being shared and explain why sometimes what you see online isn’t always what it claims to be.

title: BBC Verify: US-Israel war with Iran sees AI fakes and disinformation spread online
paragraph: BBC Verify is also assessing the ongoing risks to shipping in the Strait of Hormuz through which around 20% of the world's oil passes. Three vessels were attacked near the strait yesterday and ships have been warned not to attempt to transit the key route.

title: This Video of Mahindra Thar Stuck on Signboard on Expressway Is AI-Generated
paragraph: A video which shows a Mahindra Thar stuck on an overhead signboard is being shared on social media platforms with several users claiming that the incident recent took place on the National Highway (NH)-48.

title: This Video of Mahindra Thar Stuck on Signboard on Expressway Is AI-Generated
paragraph: What did the viral post say?: It was posted with a caption that said, "Malayali virus everywhere, Welcome to mahindra airlines.! (sic)."

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: In this exclusive conversation with Outlook, Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh, Vice-Chancellor of Guru Nanak Dev University, outlines an ambitious vision for reimagining public higher education in India. At the heart of his tenure lies a bold integration of regional identity and technological advancement — from the “Punjabi First” initiative to the establishment of the Surjit Patar Ethical Centre for AI. Rooted in the moral philosophy of Guru Nanak Dev, his approach seeks to harmonise language, ethics, and innovation in an AI-driven era. Addressing issues ranging from employability and research impact to student well-being and global competitiveness, he presents a roadmap for transforming a public university into a nationally recognised model of value-based, innovation-led education.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: If I must identify one defining reform, it would be the structural repositioning of Guru Nanak Dev University as a value-rooted, language-empowered, ethically driven modern university through the twin initiatives of “Punjabi First” and the establishment of the Surjit Patar Ethical Centre for AI.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: The Act of Guru Nanak Dev University is not merely an administrative document; it carries a moral and cultural mandate — to promote Punjabi language, preserve regional heritage, and uphold the ethical values inspired by Guru Nanak Dev Ji. Over time, like many institutions, universities tend to drift toward standardisation and metric-driven competition. My conviction has been that global excellence and regional identity are not opposites — they must reinforce each other.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: The “Punjabi First” initiative is therefore not linguistic nostalgia. It is a structural reform. It ensures that Punjabi becomes a functional academic, research, and technological language in administration, digital content, AI training datasets, and skill development. If Punjab is to participate meaningfully in the AI age, our language must not remain confined to literature alone — it must enter code, computation, governance, and innovation.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: Parallel to this, we have conceptualised the Surjit Patar Ethical Centre for AI, named after one of Punjab’s finest literary minds. Artificial Intelligence is reshaping education, governance, and society. But technology without ethics can deepen inequality and algorithmic bias. Inspired by Guru Nanak Dev Ji’s principles of truthfulness (Sach), humility (Nimrata), and universal welfare (Sarbat da Bhala), the Centre aims to embed ethical reasoning, transparency, and accountability within AI research and pedagogy.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: If, at the end of my tenure, Guru Nanak Dev University is recognised nationally as a university that demonstrated how a regional language and spiritual ethos can power modern technological leadership — that will be the reform I would like to be remembered for.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: Q2. Punjab has many higher-education challenges — where do you think Guru Nanak Dev University can lead the state nationally?

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: Punjab faces three structural challenges: youth migration, skill mismatch, and overdependence on limited economic sectors.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: Sports Excellence as Institutional Strength – We are the only university in India to win the MAKA Trophy 25 times . This proves that holistic education is not rhetoric; it is measurable. Research Growth Model – Our h-index has risen from 62 in 2017 to 175 in 2025. This shows how focused research ecosystems can elevate public universities. Entrepreneurship & AI Integration – By combining AI-driven courses with start-up incubation, we aim to retain talent by creating opportunity within Punjab rather than exporting it.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: Our ambition is to make Guru Nanak Dev University a model for value-based, innovation-driven public higher education in India.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: However, while we modernise, we must not forget a fundamental truth: public universities in India have historically been the greatest engines of social mobility. They opened their doors to poor, rural, first-generation, and brilliant students — many of whom went on to bring laurels to Bharat in science, civil services, sports, literature, and industry. That democratizing role must never be diluted.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: But alongside these reforms, we anchor ourselves in the ethical framework inspired by Guru Nanak Dev Ji— Sach (truthfulness), Kirat (dignified effort), Vand Chhakna (equitable sharing), and Sarbat Da Bhala (welfare of all).

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: Reinvention must therefore be inclusive. Public universities cannot carry this responsibility alone. If India wants equitable growth, both government and industry must strengthen public institutions — because they educate the largest and most diverse segment of the population.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: Our effort at Guru Nanak Dev University is to demonstrate that a public university can be technologically advanced, globally competitive, and yet deeply committed to social justice.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: Rankings and research matter — and we are strengthening both — but the primary moral obligation of a public university is to prepare students for dignified, productive lives. India’s demographic dividend will become a liability if graduates leave campuses with degrees but without competence.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: Employability today is not just placement statistics. It means problem-solving ability, technological fluency, communication skills, ethical professionalism, and adaptability. At the same time, we are cultivating an entrepreneurial mindset so students can become job creators, not merely job seekers.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: Public universities have historically enabled poor, rural, and first-generation learners to rise and serve the nation. If industry and government truly want inclusive growth, they must invest in strengthening this ecosystem.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: When universities produce skilled, ethical, self-reliant citizens, rankings will follow naturally — but nation-building will lead the way.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: The most urgent skill gap we must address is the ability to apply knowledge to solve real-world problems with confidence, creativity, and responsibility.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: Our students are intelligent and ambitious. The DNA of Punjabi youth is defined by hard work, innovation, and resilience. Across continents — in business, agriculture, science, and public service — Punjabis have demonstrated global leadership. The challenge is not capability; it is structured preparation.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: Universities must prepare students not only for Punjab, not only for India — but for the world. However, global leadership does not emerge in abstraction. It is built through solving concrete problems.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: Punjab today faces critical issues — declining groundwater levels, sustainable agriculture, diversified irrigation models, rural enterprise transformation. If students can design solutions for these complex challenges, they will be equipped to lead anywhere in the world.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: NEP-2020 rightly emphasises multidisciplinary learning, experiential education, and skill integration. We must translate that into project-based learning, industry immersion, agri-tech innovation labs, interdisciplinary problem centres, and international collaborations.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: We must teach students to think globally, compete globally, and lead globally — while remaining capable of innovating for their soil.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: For too long, research in many universities has been evaluated by publications alone. Our approach is different — research must demonstrate measurable social and industrial relevance.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: First, I have directed all Deans and Research Boards that while approving PhD and funded research proposals, the potential social, economic, or technological impact of the research must be clearly articulated. Research topics are now being evaluated not only for academic novelty, but for societal relevance — particularly in areas such as sustainable agriculture, groundwater management, renewable energy, public health, and sports science.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: Second, we have strengthened our IPR and Patent Support ecosystem to ensure ideas move from laboratory to protection and commercialization.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: Third, through the Golden Jubilee Centre for Entrepreneurship and Innovation, promising research outcomes are incubated into start-ups, bridging academia and enterprise.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: Additionally, we are actively engaging with foreign universities for joint research publications and collaborative projects, ensuring global standards while addressing local needs.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: However, universities cannot do this alone. For research to truly transform society, industry and government must partner proactively — through funded projects, shared laboratories, pilot implementation platforms, and innovation grants.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: This year, students will experience AI-assisted learning modules, interdisciplinary technology-driven projects, recorded and hybrid lectures for flexibility, and faculty who are being systematically trained to use AI as a pedagogical tool rather than a shortcut.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: We have roped in our distinguished alumni — many of whom are working in leading global technology firms — and constituted a Strategic Advisory Board that includes Indian and international AI experts. They are actively guiding us on how to implement new ideas in ways that are academically rigorous, socially responsible, and contextually relevant.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: AI will supplement, not replace, human mentorship. We are embedding ethics, critical thinking, and accountability into AI education so students learn not just how to use technology, but how to use it responsibly.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: Public universities today face a structural funding challenge. However, the solution is not commercialization — it is strategic diversification with autonomy.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: One significant example is the generous contribution of our USA-based alumnus, Sardar Jaspreet Singh, Attorney, who has established Sikh Studies Chairs at the university. This diaspora-supported endowment demonstrates how alumni can become institutional nation-builders.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: With his support, we have also conceptualized innovative academic offerings such as an AI-based certificate course titled “Punjabi for Non-Punjabi Speaking People”, aimed at global learners, as well as structured courses on Sikh history and civilizational studies. These programs not only preserve heritage but also create new academic revenue streams through global digital outreach.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: Public universities must be supported by government, strengthened by industry collaboration, and empowered by alumni philanthropy — so they can continue to provide equitable access to quality education.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: We are the only university in India to have won the prestigious MAKA Trophy 25 times, reflecting sustained excellence in sports. Participation in athletics, team events, and national competitions creates resilience, confidence, and peer bonding — essential foundations for mental well-being. Similarly, our strong performance in youth cultural festivals nurtures creativity and emotional expression.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: Academically, we have developed a mentorship-driven culture and structured policies — including our first-ever comprehensive sports policy — ensuring fairness and clarity.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: Our Psychology Department is now exploring structured counselling outreach in Punjab’s border districts, in collaboration with district administration, recognising the region’s unique social pressures.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: Beyond systems, we organise melas, seminars, and workshops that promote ethical reflection and discipline. Inspired by Guru Nanak Dev Ji’s message — “Thaal vich tin vastu paio: Sat, Santokh, Vicharo” (Truth, Contentment, Contemplation) — we encourage students to cultivate balance in a high-pressure world.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: In five years, I would want you to see a university that has moved from incremental progress to structural transformation.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: First, I would like Guru Nanak Dev University to be recognised as a national model of a value-driven, innovation-led public university — where Punjabi language, ethical foundations inspired by Guru Nanak Dev Ji, and cutting-edge AI integration coexist seamlessly.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: Most importantly, I would want Guru Nanak Dev University to stand as proof that a public university can modernise without losing its moral compass.

title: Prof. (Dr.) Karamjeet Singh On Ethics, AI, And “Punjabi First” At Guru Nanak Dev University
paragraph: If we can demonstrate that technology can serve society, language can power innovation, and education can uplift the most deserving — that will be meaningful transformation.

title: AI developers should counter misinformation, protect fact-based news: Global media groups
paragraph: A global group of broadcasters and publishers is calling on artificial intelligence developers to make sure their technology serves the public by helping to counter misinformation and protect the value of fact-based news.

title: AI developers should counter misinformation, protect fact-based news: Global media groups
paragraph: The Geneva-based European Broadcasting Union, an association of public broadcasters that's also known for running the popular Eurovision Song Contest, says it and the World Association of News Publishers and other partners want to cooperate with tech companies behind AI.

title: AI developers should counter misinformation, protect fact-based news: Global media groups
paragraph: The group says thousands of public and private media in broadcast, print and online formats have joined the “News Integrity in the Age of AI” initiative, whose five core steps were announced Monday at the World News Media Congress in Krakow, Poland.

title: AI developers should counter misinformation, protect fact-based news: Global media groups
paragraph: The initiative is calling for news content to only be used in generative AI models with the authorisation of the content originator, and for clarity about attribution and accuracy. It says the original news source behind AI-generated material must be “apparent and accessible."

title: AI developers should counter misinformation, protect fact-based news: Global media groups
paragraph: “Organisations and institutions that see truth and facts as the desirable core of a democracy and the foundation of an empowered society should now come together at one table to shape the next era,” said Ladina Heimgartner, president of the publishers association and CEO of Switzerland’s Ringier Media.

title: AI developers should counter misinformation, protect fact-based news: Global media groups
paragraph: Among the media affiliates along with EBU and WAN-IFRA — as the association of news publishers is known — are Latin American broadcasters association AIL; the Asia-Pacific Broadcasting Union; and the North American Broadcasters Association, which counts Fox, Paramount, NBC Universal and PBS among its members.

title: AI developers should counter misinformation, protect fact-based news: Global media groups
paragraph: Since AI surged into public awareness with the launch of OpenAI’s ChatGPT in November 2022, legacy media have grappled with how best to use the technology, and whether to cooperate with or even combat the companies behind it.

title: AI developers should counter misinformation, protect fact-based news: Global media groups
paragraph: The New York Times and other newspapers have filed a copyright lawsuit against OpenAI and business partner Microsoft. The Times has said the tech companies have threatened its livelihood by effectively stealing billions of dollars’ worth of work by its journalists.

title: AI developers should counter misinformation, protect fact-based news: Global media groups
paragraph: Many news outlets have struck deals with OpenAI, including the Associated Press. which has a licensing and technology deal with it as well as with Google for delivery of news through its Gemini AI chatbot.

title: AI developers should counter misinformation, protect fact-based news: Global media groups
paragraph: In the U.S., leading tech companies like Google, Microsoft and OpenAI have told the Copyright Office that their training of AI models fits into the “fair use” doctrine that allows for limited uses of copyrighted materials. That includes teaching, research or transforming the copyrighted work into something different.

title: Govt tightens rules on AI generated content, mandates labels and faster takedowns
paragraph: The government has tightened India’s internet rules to bring AI-generated and deepfake content under stricter compliance, mandating clear labelling, permanent metadata and faster takedown timelines for online platforms.

title: Govt tightens rules on AI generated content, mandates labels and faster takedowns
paragraph: The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) on February 10 notified the Information Technology (Intermediary Guidelines and Digital Media Ethics Code) Amendment Rules, 2026, expanding the scope of the law to explicitly cover what it calls “synthetically generated information”.

title: Govt tightens rules on AI generated content, mandates labels and faster takedowns
paragraph: The notification defines synthetically generated information as audio, visual or audio-visual content that is “artificially or algorithmically created, generated, modified or altered using a computer resource” in a way that makes it appear authentic.

title: Govt tightens rules on AI generated content, mandates labels and faster takedowns
paragraph: “‘Synthetically generated information’ means audio, visual or audio-visual information which is artificially or algorithmically created, generated, modified or altered using a computer resource, in a manner that such information appears to be real, authentic or true,” the rules state.

title: Govt tightens rules on AI generated content, mandates labels and faster takedowns
paragraph: However, the government clarified that routine editing, formatting, technical correction or the good-faith creation of documents, PDFs, research outputs and educational material will not be treated as synthetic content.

title: Govt tightens rules on AI generated content, mandates labels and faster takedowns
paragraph: Under the amended framework, intermediaries that enable the creation or dissemination of AI-generated content will be required to prominently label such content.

title: Govt tightens rules on AI generated content, mandates labels and faster takedowns
paragraph: “Every such information… shall be prominently labelled in a manner that ensures prominent visibility… that can be used to immediately identify that such information is synthetically generated information,” the notification said.

title: Govt tightens rules on AI generated content, mandates labels and faster takedowns
paragraph: Platforms must also embed permanent metadata or technical provenance mechanisms, including a unique identifier, to trace the computer resource used to generate or modify the content.

title: Govt tightens rules on AI generated content, mandates labels and faster takedowns
paragraph: Social media intermediaries will now have to ask users to declare whether content is AI-generated before publishing it and deploy automated tools to verify those declarations.

title: Govt tightens rules on AI generated content, mandates labels and faster takedowns
paragraph: “Where such declaration or technical verification confirms that the information is synthetically generated, ensure that the same is clearly and prominently displayed with an appropriate label,” the rules say.

title: Govt tightens rules on AI generated content, mandates labels and faster takedowns
paragraph: If a platform is found to have knowingly allowed such content in violation of the rules, it will be deemed to have failed its due diligence obligations, potentially risking its legal safe-harbour protection.

title: Govt tightens rules on AI generated content, mandates labels and faster takedowns
paragraph: The amendments also shorten takedown timelines. Intermediaries must now act on certain lawful orders within three hours, instead of the earlier 36 hours.

title: Govt tightens rules on AI generated content, mandates labels and faster takedowns
paragraph: User grievance redressal timelines have also been tightened, with response periods reduced from 15 days to seven days, and certain actions required within two hours.

title: Govt tightens rules on AI generated content, mandates labels and faster takedowns
paragraph: The rules explicitly prohibit AI-generated content that includes child sexual abuse material, non-consensual intimate imagery, false documents, or misleading depictions of real individuals or events.

title: Govt tightens rules on AI generated content, mandates labels and faster takedowns
paragraph: Such violations can lead to immediate removal, suspension or termination of user accounts, disclosure of user identity to victims, and mandatory reporting to law enforcement under applicable criminal laws.

title: AI at war: Artificial intelligence is reshaping defence strategies
paragraph: As the global landscape becomes increasingly volatile, from the trenches of Ukraine to the mountainous terrain of Ladakh, and from the conflict zone of Gaza to the standoff in the East over Taiwan, artificial intelligence (AI) has quietly become central to defence strategies around the world. It is no longer just a futuristic promise; AI is actively shaping how nations detect, deter and respond to threats in real-time.

title: Generative AI: 5 trends that will impact the future of OTT and video streaming platforms
paragraph: Generative AI is driving a wave of awe and hype across the world these days. We have all been amazed by the phenomenal capabilities of ChatGPT. In visual space, there is hardly anyone whose social media feeds have been immune to the artistic creativity of the Midjourney AI tool rolling out captivating images of people in different multiverses.

title: Generative AI: 5 trends that will impact the future of OTT and video streaming platforms
paragraph: The media and entertainment industry is also experiencing a shift with the advent of generative AI. This transformative technology is revolutionising the way content is created, distributed, and consumed, presenting a unique opportunity for first movers to gain a competitive edge.

title: Generative AI: 5 trends that will impact the future of OTT and video streaming platforms
paragraph: In this article, we will explore five key trends that are set to shape the future of OTT and video streaming platforms, showcasing real-life examples of how GenerativeAI is driving innovation and unlocking potential and opportunities for those in the media and entertainment space.

title: Generative AI: 5 trends that will impact the future of OTT and video streaming platforms
paragraph: Generative AI is empowering individuals with original story ideas, enabling them to build content faster and more efficiently. This trend opens doors for citizen content creators who can harness the power of AI algorithms to improve their storytelling techniques and enhance post-production processes.

title: Generative AI: 5 trends that will impact the future of OTT and video streaming platforms
paragraph: Platforms like TikTok have witnessed an explosion of user-generated content, showcasing the creativity and impact of citizen content creators. For example, the AI-powered video editing tools available on TikTok allow users to transform their videos into engaging and professionally edited content, attracting millions of viewers and turning ordinary individuals into influential content creators.

title: Generative AI: 5 trends that will impact the future of OTT and video streaming platforms
paragraph: Generative AI significantly reduces the cost and barriers to testing and experimenting with newer experiences, content formats, and visuals. This trend enables content creators to explore uncharted territories and push creative boundaries.

title: Generative AI: 5 trends that will impact the future of OTT and video streaming platforms
paragraph: Netflix, known for its data-driven approach, leverages generative AI algorithms to understand viewer preferences and create personalised content recommendations. By analysing user behaviour and content consumption patterns, Netflix tailors its offerings, resulting in fresh perspectives and captivating experiences.

title: Generative AI: 5 trends that will impact the future of OTT and video streaming platforms
paragraph: Netflix in the past has fostered creativity, delivering unique and immersive narratives and delivered movies like Black Mirror: Bandersnatch, You Vs Wild, Cat Burglar, Animals on the Loose etc. after years of experimentation. Get ready for a faster lead time on unique experiences in the streaming space.

title: Generative AI: 5 trends that will impact the future of OTT and video streaming platforms
paragraph: One of the advantages of generative AI is its ability to enhance user experiences. Through personalised recommendations, enhanced content discovery, and seamless operations, generative AI drives higher engagement, viewer satisfaction, and platform growth.

title: Generative AI: 5 trends that will impact the future of OTT and video streaming platforms
paragraph: Spotify, the popular music streaming platform, utilises AI algorithms to curate personalised playlists based on individual preferences, listening history, and contextual factors. By providing tailored music recommendations, Spotify offers an enhanced user experience, keeping listeners engaged and loyal to the platform.

title: Generative AI: 5 trends that will impact the future of OTT and video streaming platforms
paragraph: With generative AI algorithms, understanding user requirements will improve due to better cognitive recognition and hyper-personalisation strategies will see more efficacy, resulting in longer viewing sessions and increased platform usage.

title: Generative AI: 5 trends that will impact the future of OTT and video streaming platforms
paragraph: Generative AI can analyse vast amounts of data related to user behaviour, purchase history, demographics, and market trends. By identifying patterns and correlations, it can uncover hidden insights that can inform monetisation strategies. By simulating various pricing scenarios and predicting customer responses, it can even identify pricing strategies that maximise revenue or profit margins.

title: Generative AI: 5 trends that will impact the future of OTT and video streaming platforms
paragraph: Furthermore, generative AI enables the creation of novel and innovative content formats. For example, it can generate virtual reality experiences, interactive narratives, or personalised advertisements. These new content formats can attract a broader audience and open up additional revenue streams through licensing, partnerships, or premium offerings.

title: Generative AI: 5 trends that will impact the future of OTT and video streaming platforms
paragraph: Generative AI enables the entertainment industry to meet the evolving demands of audiences by producing a wider range of content that caters to diverse interests and preferences. The collaboration between generative AI and content creators will ensure a seamless content supply chain.

title: Generative AI: 5 trends that will impact the future of OTT and video streaming platforms
paragraph: Faster production cycles, improved processes, rapid experimentation, and an overall improved understanding of customer behaviour and expectations combined with the liberalisation of content creation will create a continuous supply to keep users engaged and involved.

title: Generative AI: 5 trends that will impact the future of OTT and video streaming platforms
paragraph: In a nutshell, generative AI presents an opportunity for first movers in the media and entertainment industry. By embracing the trends of citizen content creators, fostering experiences and creativity, improving user experiences, optimising monetisation strategies, and enhancing the content supply chain, businesses can gain a competitive edge and position themselves as leaders in the ever-evolving digital landscape. Real-life examples from platforms like TikTok, Netflix, Spotify, Disney, and Amper Music showcase the tangible benefits of incorporating generative AI into the media and entertainment space.

title: Generative AI: 5 trends that will impact the future of OTT and video streaming platforms
paragraph: As the industry continues to evolve, those who seize the potential of generative AI will undoubtedly shape the future of OTT and video streaming platforms, providing an entertainment experience to audiences worldwide.

title: Instagram CEO warns: The AI content boom is eroding authenticity
paragraph: As artificial intelligence rapidly reshapes how images and videos are created, Instagram CEO Adam Mosseri is warning of a future where telling what is real online will no longer be straightforward.

title: Instagram CEO warns: The AI content boom is eroding authenticity
paragraph: In a series of end-of-year posts shared on Instagram and Threads, Mosseri reflected on the state of photos, videos, and authenticity on social media amid what he described as a growing flood of AI-generated content. At the heart of his message is a clear concern: the signals people once relied on to trust what they see online are beginning to break down.

title: Instagram CEO warns: The AI content boom is eroding authenticity
paragraph: According to Mosseri, one of the biggest risks Instagram faces is not just competition or changing formats, but the speed at which the world itself is evolving. Looking ahead to 2026, he points to a major shift already underway. “Authenticity is becoming infinitely reproducible,” he wrote.

title: Instagram CEO warns: The AI content boom is eroding authenticity
paragraph: “Everything that made creators matter – the ability to be real, to connect, to have a voice that couldn’t be faked – is now accessible to anyone with the right tools,” Mosseri said. With deepfakes improving rapidly and AI now capable of generating photos and videos that are increasingly indistinguishable from captured media, the idea of visual proof is weakening.

title: Instagram CEO warns: The AI content boom is eroding authenticity
paragraph: Mosseri connects this shift to the internet’s earlier power transfer from institutions to individuals once the cost of distributing information dropped to nearly zero. “Individuals, not publishers or brands, established that there’s a significant market for content from people,” he noted.

title: Instagram CEO warns: The AI content boom is eroding authenticity
paragraph: At the same time, trust in institutions is at an all-time low. As a result, people have increasingly turned to self-captured content from creators they trust and admire. While frustration around what is often labelled “AI slop” is widespread, Mosseri argued that not all AI content is low quality. “There’s a lot of amazing AI content,” he said.

title: Instagram CEO warns: The AI content boom is eroding authenticity
paragraph: “Even the quality AI content has a look,” Mosseri observed, pointing to overly smooth skin and overly slick visuals. That, he warned, is temporary. “That will change. We're going to see more realistic AI content.”

title: Instagram CEO warns: The AI content boom is eroding authenticity
paragraph: Rather than making creators irrelevant, Mosseri believes this shift could make authenticity more valuable. As it becomes harder to tell what is real, demand for trusted voices may grow. “The bar is shifting from ‘can you create?’ to ‘can you make something that only you could create?’” he wrote.

title: Instagram CEO warns: The AI content boom is eroding authenticity
paragraph: Mosseri also challenged long-held perceptions of Instagram itself. For many users, particularly those over 25, the platform is still associated with a feed of polished, square photos. “That feed is dead,” he stated.

title: Instagram CEO warns: The AI content boom is eroding authenticity
paragraph: He explained that people stopped sharing personal moments on the public feed years ago. Today, the primary mode of sharing is through direct messages. “Blurry photos and shaky videos of daily experiences. Shoe shots and unflattering candids now dominate how users document their lives."

title: Instagram CEO warns: The AI content boom is eroding authenticity
paragraph: This raw aesthetic has increasingly spilt into public content. In that context, Mosseri believes parts of the imaging and camera industries are optimised for the wrong looks. “They’re competing to make everyone look like a pro photographer from 2015,” he said.

title: Instagram CEO warns: The AI content boom is eroding authenticity
paragraph: “Flattering imagery is cheap to produce and boring to consume,” Mosseri argued. Instead, imperfection has become meaningful. “In a world where everything can be perfected, imperfection becomes a signal.”

title: Instagram CEO warns: The AI content boom is eroding authenticity
paragraph: Rawness, he added, is no longer just an aesthetic choice. “Rawness isn’t just aesthetic preference anymore; it's proof. It’s defensive. A way of saying, “This is real because it’s imperfect.”

title: Instagram CEO warns: The AI content boom is eroding authenticity
paragraph: However, Mosseri does not see this approach as a permanent solution. AI, he acknowledged, will eventually be able to generate any aesthetic, including imperfect ones that convincingly appear authentic. When that happens, the focus will need to shift again. “We’ll need to shift our focus to who says something instead of what is being said.”

title: Instagram CEO warns: The AI content boom is eroding authenticity
paragraph: Reflecting on the pace of change, Mosseri noted that for most of his life, photos and videos could largely be assumed to be accurate records of real moments. “This is clearly no longer the case, and it’s going to take us years to adapt,” he wrote.

title: Instagram CEO warns: The AI content boom is eroding authenticity
paragraph: He expects users to move from default belief to default scepticism. Instead of assuming what they see is real, people will increasingly question who is sharing something and why. “This will be uncomfortable. We're genetically predisposed to believing our eyes,” he said.

title: Instagram CEO warns: The AI content boom is eroding authenticity
paragraph: Platforms like Instagram, Mosseri added, will continue working to identify and label AI-generated content. But as AI improves, that task will get harder. “It will be more practical to fingerprint real media than fake media,” he suggested, pointing to solutions like cryptographic image signing at capture.

title: Instagram CEO warns: The AI content boom is eroding authenticity
paragraph: Ultimately, Mosseri argued that labels alone will not be enough. “We need to surface much more context about the accounts sharing content so people can make informed decisions,” he wrote.

title: Instagram CEO warns: The AI content boom is eroding authenticity
paragraph: In a world of “infinite abundance and infinite doubt”, he believes the creators who stand out will be those who can maintain trust by being real, transparent and consistent.
